explanation: It consist of alternate layers of hard & soft rocks which are arranged horizontally.

subject: Geography
q: The most common tree in the desert is the
explanation: The most common tree in the desert is the Acacia. Acacia trees are well adapted to desert conditions, they have long roots that can reach deep water sources, thick bark to resist fire, and small, waxy leaves to reduce water loss.

subject: Geography
q: The most obvious physical feature that distinguishes undisturbed mature soil from an imature soil is the
explanation: The most obvious physical feature that distinguishes undisturbed mature soil from immature soil is the layered profile. Mature soils have a clear layered structure, with a topsoil layer rich in organic matter, a subsoil layer where minerals are leached down from the topsoil, and a parent material layer which is the original rock material that the soil formed from.

subject: Geography
q: The direction of flow of ocean currents is determined mainly by the
options: rotation of the earth and land and sea breeze
shape of the continents and the temperature of the continental interior
prevailing winds and the rotation of the earth
depth of the ocean and the amount of fresh water inflow
explanation: Earth rotation deflects ocean to the right in the northern hemisphere and to the left in the southern hemisphere.

subject: Geography
q: The medium class of layered cloud which brings continuous rain, snow or sleet is the
explanation: Nimbostratus clouds are a type of cloud that cover the sky in a dark gray layer. They are thick enough to block out the sun and are often associated with continuous rain, snow, or sleet. Therefore, the correct answer is nimbostratus.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: The biodiversity of an ecosystem may be reduced through the
explanation: Biodiversity refers to the variety of life in a particular habitat or ecosystem. It can be reduced through the extinction of plant species. When a species goes extinct, it can disrupt the food chain and lead to a decrease in biodiversity. Therefore, the correct answer is the extinction of plant species.

subject: Geography
q: A soft, black opaque carbonaceous rock that can be used to make pencil is
explanation: Graphite is a soft, black, crystalline form of carbon that is used for making pencils. It is opaque and has a metallic luster. Therefore, the correct answer is graphite.

subject: Geography
q: The vegetation that is characterized by tall trees, climbing plants and creepers in the Amazon is called
explanation: The vegetation characterized by tall trees, climbing plants, and creepers in the Amazon is called 'selvas'. This term is used to describe the dense, tropical rainforest that is found in the Amazon Basin. The other options, pampas, veldt, and steppes, refer to different types of grasslands, not rainforests.

subject: Geography
q: One predominantly temperate grain that is being cultivated in some tropical areas today is
explanation: Maize and Sorghum are predominant in the tropics while barley and wheat are for temperate regions but wheat is becoming more adaptable to the tropics.

subject: Geography
q: The scientist who propounded the theory of continental drift was
explanation:
He propounded the theory of how the super continent is divided into various lands.
Continental drift is the movement of the Earth's continents relative to each other, thus appearing to drift across the ocean bed. The speculation that continents might have 'drifted' was first put forward by Abraham Ortelius in 1596. The concept was independently and more fully developed by Alfred Wegener in 1912.

subject: Geography
q: A sedimentary formed only in sea and oceans in warm tropical and subtropical environments is
explanation: Forms at the bottom of water bodies like oceans and seas, especially in quiet places.

subject: Geography
q: Exfoliation and rock disintegration are wealthering processes mostly found in the
explanation: Exfoliation occurs when a rock surface is exposed to heat in the day and cold at night. This is a characteristic of desert weather.

subject: Geography
q: An area of the world that is likely to have 24-hour sunshine on July of any year is the
explanation: At that season of the year, places north of tropic of cancer experience increase in hours of daylight, northward.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: Where winds are onshore, warm ocean currents will bring a type of condition to the coastland that is
explanation: It is warm because the current is warm and wet due to the fact that current picks up water as it flows over the seas & drops it as it rains on the continent.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: The speed of the wind is usually measured by
explanation: The speed of the wind is measured using an instrument called an anemometer. A speedometer is used to measure the speed of a vehicle, an arrow is a projectile, and a wind vane is used to determine the direction of the wind, not its speed. Therefore, the correct answer is 'an anemometer'.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: Acid lava is rich in
explanation: Acid lava is high in silica content. Silica is a compound made of silicon and oxygen and is the most abundant compound in the Earth's crust. Acid lava is known for its high viscosity due to its high silica content. Therefore, the correct answer is 'silica'.

subject: Geography
q: The live commentary of a football match which started at 4.00 pm, at a place on longitude 150E will be received by a listener in a town on longitude 1600W at
explanation:
Longitudinal difference = 160°W + 15°E = 175°
Since 15° = 1 hour, 175/15 = 11 hours 40 minutes.
If it is at 4pm at 15° E, then it will be [4pm or (16:00) - 11:40]hours at 16°W = 4:20a.m same day.

subject: Geography
q: During winter, Eskimos live in
explanation: Eskimos are found near the north pole. They live in Igloos in winter and in tents made from animal skin during summer.

subject: Geography
q: Johannesburg, Jos and Galhivare are similar in that they all
options: grew because of the presence of minerals
explanation: These places developed due to the discovery of mineral e.g Gold in Johannesburg and Tin & Columbite in Jos.

subject: Geography
q: The shaduf method of irrigation was practiced in
explanation: Early civilization took place in Egypt with the main focus on land cultivation by irrigation near the River Nile.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : IRRIGATION AGRICULTURE IN THE NILE AND NIGER BASIN

subject: Geography
q: Dairy farming is most notable in
explanation: Denmark is known for its high-quality dairy products and is one of the leading exporters of dairy products in the world.

subject: Geography
q: The least of the factors to be considered in the allocation of industries is
explanation: While climate can have an impact on certain industries, it is generally not as important as factors such as the availability of raw materials, transport infrastructure, and markets.

subject: Geography
q: The rise in the population of an area can be measured easily by
options: multiplying the natural increase by the net migration
subtracting the natural increase from the net migration
adding the natural increase to the net migration
dividing the natural increase by the net migration
explanation: When excess of birth over death per year is added to the balance of immigration and emigration, it indicates population increase for the year.

subject: Geography
q: Transportation is an important economic activity because it
explanation: Transportation is an important economic activity because it promotes diffusion of innovation and ideas. This is because transportation allows for the movement of goods, services, and ideas from one place to another. This movement can lead to the spread of new technologies, business practices, and cultural ideas, which can stimulate economic growth and development.

subject: Geography
q: Which of these sets enhances nucleation of rural settlement?
options: Several sources of water and wet points
explanation: Nucleation of rural settlement is enhanced by 'Fertile broad plains and natural harbours'. Nucleation in geography refers to the process of forming a nucleus or core around which a settlement develops. Fertile broad plains provide ample space and resources for agriculture, which is a primary economic activity in rural areas. Natural harbours provide access to water resources and opportunities for fishing and trade, which can also attract settlement.

subject: Geography
q: The movement of a group of people from a village to an irrigation project area may be described as
explanation: The movement of a group of people from a village to an irrigation project area is described as rural-rural migration. This is because both the village and the irrigation project area are in rural settings. Urban-rural migration would imply movement from a city to a rural area, while rural-urban migration would be the opposite. Urban-urban migration would involve moving from one city to another.

subject: Geography
q: The most important factors influencing the distribution of population in the world are
options: relief, industrial activities, agriculture and mineral resources
explanation: The most important factors influencing the distribution of population in the world are climate, relief, soils and mineral resources. Climate affects the types of activities that can be carried out in a region, and thus the number of people it can support. Relief, or the physical features of the land, can also affect population distribution, as some areas may be more habitable than others. Soils influence the types of crops that can be grown, and thus the agricultural potential of a region. Mineral resources can attract industries and thus people to a region.

subject: Geography
q: The most dreaded pest in the Cotton Belt of the U.S.A is the
explanation: It can spread so rapidly that in a season, a pair can multiply to 10 million. It was responsible for cotton belt migration Westward in the 17th century.

subject: Geography
q: The Benguela current is to Namibia what the Canaries current is to
explanation: Benguela Current is responsible for Namib desert in Namibia and Canary Current is responsible for the Sahara desert in Mauritania.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : AFRICA ON BROAD OUTLINE

subject: Geography
q: The two prominent highlands at the Nigeria-Cameroun border are
explanation: The two prominent highlands at the Nigeria-Cameroon border are the Mandara and Alantika mountains. The Mandara mountains are a volcanic range extending about 200 km along the northern part of the Cameroon-Nigeria border, while the Alantika mountains are an extension of the Adamawa Plateau in northeastern Nigeria and northern Cameroon.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : GEOGRAPHICAL REGIONS OF NIGERIA

subject: Geography
q: An important mineral deposit in South Africa which has attracted a large number of white settlers is
explanation: South Africa is known for its significant gold deposits, particularly in the Witwatersrand region. The Witwatersrand Gold Rush in the late 19th century led to a large influx of white settlers in the region. Therefore, an important mineral deposit in South Africa which has attracted a large number of white settlers is gold in Witwatersrand.

subject: Geography
q: The prosperity of Kenya depends upon the cultivation of
explanation: These are crops that are grown in upland areas. The prosperity of Kenya depends upon the cultivation of coffee, maize and sisal

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : PLANTATION AGRICULTURE IN WEST AND EAST AFRICA

subject: Geography
q: One major feature of manufacturing in West Africa is that the industries
explanation: Manufacturing industries in West Africa primarily produce for the domestic market. This is due to several factors including the high cost of exporting, lack of access to international markets, and the need to meet local demand for goods.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : PLANTATION AGRICULTURE IN WEST AND EAST AFRICA

subject: Geography
q: The most important air masses which affects the climate of Nigeria are the
options: tropical maritime and tropical sea and land breezes
explanation: The climate of Nigeria is significantly influenced by two main air masses: the tropical maritime and the tropical continental. The tropical maritime air mass brings in moisture from the Atlantic Ocean, causing the rainy season, while the tropical continental air mass is dry and comes from the Sahara Desert, causing the dry season.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : GEOGRAPHICAL REGIONS OF NIGERIA

subject: Geography
q: The low rainfall along Cape Three Points in Ghana is due to the
explanation: The low rainfall along Cape Three Points in Ghana is due to the prevalence of south west winds.

subject: Geography
q: In Nigeria, continentality poses the greatest hinderance to economic development in the
explanation: In Nigeria, continentality poses the greatest hindrance to economic development in the Chad Basin. Continentality refers to the climatic condition of being far from the moderating influence of the sea, leading to greater temperature extremes. The Chad Basin, being located in the interior of the country and far from the sea, experiences extreme temperature variations which can hinder economic activities such as agriculture.

subject: Geography
q: In Nigeria, the dominant trees of the Guinea Savannah include
explanation: In Nigeria, the dominant trees of the Guinea Savannah include Isoberlinia, shea butter and silk cotton. The Guinea Savannah is a tropical grassland biome with a wet and dry climate, and these tree species are well adapted to these conditions. Isoberlinia is a genus of legume in the family Fabaceae, shea butter tree produces nuts that are used to make shea butter, a common ingredient in skin care products, and silk cotton tree is known for its large fruits filled with silky fibers.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : GEOGRAPHICAL REGIONS OF NIGERIA

subject: Geography
q: From its source to its mouth, the River Niger passes through
explanation: The River Niger is the third-longest river in Africa, exceeded only by the Nile and the Congo River (also known as the Zaire River). It runs in a crescent through Guinea, Mali, Niger and Nigeria before discharging through a massive delta, known as the Niger Delta of the Oil Rivers, into the Gulf of Guinea in the Atlantic Ocean. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Guinea, Mali, Niger and Nigeria'.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : GEOGRAPHICAL REGIONS OF NIGERIA

subject: Geography
q: An environmental hazard which occurs slowly and unnoticed in the oil-producing region of the Niger Delta of Nigeria is
explanation: The Niger Delta region of Nigeria is known for its oil production. The extraction and production of oil in this region have led to significant environmental pollution, including water, air and soil pollution. This pollution occurs slowly and often goes unnoticed until the effects are severe. Therefore, the correct answer is 'pollotion'.

subject: Geography
q: One basic mineral that is common to Sokoto, Ewekoro, Calaba and Nkalagu is
explanation: Sokoto, Ewekoro, Calaba and Nkalagu are all known for their limestone deposits. Limestone is a basic mineral used in the production of cement. Therefore, the correct answer is 'limestone'.

explanation: A dot map is the most suitable statistical diagram to show spatial distribution of population. This is because a dot map uses a dot symbol to show the presence of a feature or phenomenon. Dot maps rely on a visual scatter to show spatial pattern. In the context of population distribution, each dot could represent a certain number of people in a specific area, thus effectively showing the spatial distribution of population.

options: requires little or no skill to use
explanation: Plotting details can be compared with the actual objects right on the field to ascertain high accuracy levels as the survey progresses.

explanation: Offset lines are made via the survey line to avoid the obstacle & then continue work at the other side of the obstacle.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY I : ELEMENTARY SURVEYING CHAIN AND PRISMATIC

subject: Geography
q: The main purpose of any ground survey is to
options: measure and record lines and angles of places
derive accurate baseline data for urban planning
have a detailed knowledge of the geology of a place
make an accurate map of a part of the earth's surface
explanation: The main purpose of any ground survey is to make an accurate map of a part of the earth's surface. Ground surveys are conducted to collect data about the earth's surface. This data is then used to create detailed maps. These maps can be used for a variety of purposes, including urban planning, construction, and navigation.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY I : ELEMENTARY SURVEYING CHAIN AND PRISMATIC

subject: Geography
q: High tides are produced when the
options: sea level rises because of the earth's rotation
earth, the moon and the sun are on a straight line
moon is perpendicular to the earth's axis
earth completes a revolution round the sun
explanation: Tide is the rise in the sea level about twice in a day due to gravitational pull as a result of the earth's rotation.

subject: Geography
q: The time difference between a place on longitude 350W and another on longitude 250E is
explanation:
Longitudinal difference = 35° + 25° = 60°
Since 15° = 1 hour, 60° = 4 hours
The time difference is 4 hours.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : THE EARTH AS A PLANET

subject: Geography
q: The formation of coal is associated with
explanation: Coal is formed from the remains of plants that lived and died about 100 to 400 million years ago when parts of the Earth were covered with huge swampy forests. Coal is classified as a nonrenewable energy source because it takes millions of years to form. The energy we get from coal today comes from the energy that plants absorbed from the sun millions of years ago. The process of coal formation is associated with heat and pressure.

subject: Geography
q: An igneous rock with distinct grains of mica, feldspar and quartz is
explanation: Granite is a light-colored igneous rock with grains large enough to be visible with the unaided eye. It forms from the slow crystallization of magma below Earth's surface. Granite is composed mainly of quartz and feldspar with minor amounts of mica, amphiboles, and other minerals.

subject: Geography
q: An example of a land form produced by glaciation is
explanation: A cirque is a landform that is formed by the process of glaciation. It is a bowl-shaped depression with steep sides that is situated high on mountains. Cirques are formed when a glacier erodes the landscape, creating a rounded, concave shape. As the glacier moves, it grinds at the underlying rock, deepening the cirque.

subject: Geography
q: A deposition land form occurring where streams emerge from a mountain area in a desert is
explanation: From the options, self dune is the only depositional land from in the desert.

subject: Geography
q: The Dead Sea is the most saline lake in the world because
explanation: The dead sea in Palestine desert is surrounded with rocks of high salt content.

subject: Geography
q: The Stevenson's screen is raised about one metre above the ground in the order to avoid
explanation: The Stevenson's screen is raised about one metre above the ground to avoid the heat from the ground. The screen is used to house meteorological instruments, such as thermometers, to protect them from precipitation and direct heat radiation from outside sources, while still allowing air to circulate freely around them. The height is to avoid the influence of heat radiated from the ground surface which could affect the readings.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: High temperatures and heavy rainfall throughout the year describe the climate of
options: Britain, North and South Korea and Namibia
Democratic Republic of Congo, Brazil and indonesia
explanation: High temperature and heavy rainfall all year round are charateristics of equitorial climate, the areas where D.R.C, Brazil and Indonesia are located.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : AFRICA ON BROAD OUTLINE

subject: Geography
q: The variable which is both a control and an element of weather and climate is
explanation: Temperature is both a control and an element of weather and climate. As a control, temperature influences other weather elements such as humidity, pressure, and wind. For instance, temperature differences can cause changes in air pressure, which in turn affects wind patterns. As an element, temperature is a fundamental aspect of weather and climate, with daily and seasonal temperature patterns defining different types of climates.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: The prairies of North America are
explanation: The prairies of North America are temperate grasslands. Temperate grasslands, also known as prairies in North America, are characterized by a lack of trees, except for those found growing along rivers or streams, and a climate that has a range of temperatures and precipitation. The prairies of North America are known for their fertile soils which make them ideal for farming.

subject: Geography
q: The vertical section of the soil from the surface down to the bedrock, showing different layers, is known as soil
explanation: The vertical section of the soil from the surface down to the bedrock, showing different layers, is known as soil profile. A soil profile is a vertical cross-section of the soil, made of layers, generally parallel to the surface, differing from each other but having common properties.

subject: Geography
q: The greatest threat to the world's wildlife population is
explanation: Poor management of game reserves leads to excessive hunting.

subject: Geography
q: Bees and butterflies are important to flowering plants because they
explanation: Bees and butterflies are important to flowering plants because they pollinate the plants. Pollination is the act of transferring pollen grains from the male anther of a flower to the female stigma. This is crucial for the fertilization of plants.

subject: Geography
q: A hazard that could be traced directly to ozone layer depletion is the
options: introduction of ultra violet rays into the troposphere
escalation of the melting of polar and temperate ice caps
extension of desert-like conditions of the savanna
submergence of numerous coastal settlements in the world
explanation: The ozone layer acts as a shield that absorbs most of the Sun's harmful ultraviolet (UV) rays. When the ozone layer is depleted, more UV rays can get through, leading to an increased risk for skin cancer and cataracts in humans, and potential damage to plant life. Therefore, the introduction of ultra violet rays into the troposphere is a hazard that could be traced directly to ozone layer depletion.

subject: Geography
q: The population of a town in 1975 was 210,000 but grew to 280,000 in 1980. What was the annual growth rate?
explanation:
(Ppresent - Ppast)/Ppast × 100
= [(280,000 - 210,000)/210,000] × 100
= 33.33%

subject: Geography
q: The sex ratio of any population may be affected by
explanation: The state of being mortal, the condition of being subject to death.

subject: Geography
q: Nucleated groups of houses occupied largely by people engaged in primary occupations are referred to as
explanation: Village is a large concentrated rural settlement with the occupants engaging mainly in primary activities e.g farming, fishing and mining.

subject: Geography
q: The merging together of a number of urban settlements will produce a
explanation: The merging together of a number of urban settlements will produce a conurbation. A conurbation is a region comprising a number of cities, large towns, and other urban areas that, through population growth and physical expansion, have merged to form one continuous urban and industrially developed area.

subject: Geography
q: The world's leading producer of cocoa is
explanation: The world's leading producer of cocoa is Ghana. Ghana overtook its close competitor Côte d'Ivoire to become the world's largest producer of cocoa. Cocoa production occurs in the country's forested areas where rainfall is 1,000-1,500 millimeters per year.

subject: Geography
q: Automobile industries that are essentially assembly plants are found in
explanation: West African countries don't manufacture vehicles. Parts are imported for assemblage.

subject: Geography
q: Crude oil constitutes the bulk of the export products of
explanation: Qatar, Nigeria, Kuwait, and Saudi Arabia are all major oil-producing countries. Crude oil is a significant part of their export products. Libya, Qatar, Ghana, and Saudi Arabia; Australia, Java, Nigeria, and Libya; Nigeria, Cote d'lvoire, Kuwait, and Australia also export oil, but not in the same quantities or proportions as the first group.

subject: Geography
q: Western Europe and North Eastern U.S.A are the most industrialized region of the world because they
options: are on either side of the Atlantic Ocean
explanation: Each of these countries have a high level of productivity.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : INTERNATIONAL ECONOMIC COOPERATION IN WEST AFRICA ECOWAS

subject: Geography
q: The basic raw materials for the textile industry include
explanation: The basic raw materials for the textile industry include flax, wool, silk, and cotton. These materials are used to produce various types of textiles. Flax is used to produce linen, wool is used to produce woolen textiles, silk is used to produce silk textiles, and cotton is used to produce cotton textiles.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following pairs of cities have both a seaport and an international airport?
explanation: Kampala, Mombassa and Johannesburg are not hinterland countries. Warri does not have an international airport.

subject: Geography
q: The main problem faced by export crop producers in developing countries is
explanation: Insufficient capital is fundamental. The main problem faced by export crop producers in developing countries is lack of capital.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : PLANTATION AGRICULTURE IN WEST AND EAST AFRICA

subject: Geography
q: The areas in Nigeria where temperate crops may be grown include
explanation: The Jos Plateau and Mambilla Plateau are areas in Nigeria where temperate crops may be grown. This is due to their high altitude which results in a cooler climate compared to the rest of the country. Temperate crops are crops that grow in temperate climates, which are typically cooler and have distinct seasonal changes.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : PLANTATION AGRICULTURE IN WEST AND EAST AFRICA

subject: Geography
q: The greatest environmental problem around Nkalagu and Ewekoro is
explanation: The greatest environmental problem around Nkalagu and Ewekoro is air pollution. This is due to the presence of cement factories in these areas which release pollutants into the air during the production process. Air pollution is the introduction of harmful substances into the Earth's atmosphere, which can cause diseases, allergies, death to humans, damage to other living organisms such as animals and food crops, or the natural or built environment.

subject: Geography
q: The development of a climax vegetation in the savanna belt of Nigeria is disturbed mainly by
explanation: Animals depend largely on grass and that is why in dry seasons, the grasses are burned so as to grow fresh ones on time.

subject: Geography
q: Nigeria recently entered into a trade agreement in petroleum products and gas with
explanation: Oil producing nations of Africa. Nigeria recently entered into a trade agreement in petroleum products and gas with most countries of North Africa

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : INTERNATIONAL ECONOMIC COOPERATION IN WEST AFRICA ECOWAS

subject: Geography
q: The group of hardwoods that are suitable for railway sleepers in Nigeria is
explanation: The group of hardwoods that are suitable for railway sleepers in Nigeria include mahogany, obeche and iroko. These woods are known for their durability and resistance to decay, making them ideal for use in railway sleepers. Pine and fir are softwoods, not hardwoods, and gmelina and spruce are not typically used for railway sleepers in Nigeria.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : PLANTATION AGRICULTURE IN WEST AND EAST AFRICA

subject: Geography
q: A good example of cuesta landscape in Nigeria economy today is
explanation: A cuesta landscape is a hill or ridge with a gentle slope on one side, and a steep slope on the other. In Nigeria, the Udi Hills are a good example of a cuesta landscape. The other options, Yoruba Hills, North Central Highlands, and Biu Plateau, do not have the characteristic slopes of a cuesta landscape.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : GEOGRAPHICAL REGIONS OF NIGERIA

subject: Geography
q: The most capital-intensive sector of the Nigeria economy today is
explanation:
Transportation is a very large sector. It covers the different modes of transportation such as air, water, road and rail. The cost of maintaining the routes of these modes of transportation is expensive. Also the facilities such as seaport and airport is costly to maintain, same with the spare parts.
The cost of also building an airplane or ship is really expensive.

Other capital intensive companies includes the Energy companies, Automobile manufacturing companies etc

subject: Geography
q: Lakes Kivu and Malawi are related in that they
explanation: Lakes Kivu and Malawi are both rift valley lakes. A rift valley is a linear shaped lowland between several highlands or mountain ranges created by the action of a geologic rift or fault. These lakes were formed when the earth's crust cracked and weakened, causing the land to sink and fill with water.

subject: Geography
q: The relative dryness of the Horn of African is due to
options: its closeness to high ranges of East Africa
the shape and direction of the shoreline
the dominance of cold currents in the region
the distance from the belt of tropical cyclones
explanation: The coastline is aligned in the direction of the prevailing wind.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : AFRICA ON BROAD OUTLINE

subject: Geography
q: The major problems associated with rapid urban growth in tropical Africa are
options: traffic congestion, neglect of agriculture urban-rural migration
urban-urban migration, unemployment and rising crime rate
rural-urban migration, unemployment and rising crime rate
explanation: Rapid urban growth in tropical Africa is associated with rural-urban migration, unemployment, and rising crime rate. Rural-urban migration is the movement of people from rural areas to urban areas in search of better living conditions and job opportunities. This leads to overpopulation in urban areas, resulting in unemployment and a rise in crime rate due to lack of sufficient resources and job opportunities.

subject: Geography
q: Example of fold mountain in Africa are
explanation: Mount Kenya, Cameroun and Kilimajaro are volcanic mountains.

subject: Geography
q: A country well known for the production of copper in Africa is
explanation: Zambia is well known for the production of copper in Africa. The country has the largest amount of known copper reserves in Africa and its economy heavily relies on copper mining and export. The Copperbelt Province, known for its mineral deposits, is the industrial core of Zambia.

subject: Geography
q: The most densely populated city in which is also the capital of a country in Africa is
explanation: Cairo is the most densely populated city in Africa and also the capital of Egypt. The population density of a geographical region is the number of people living in that region per unit of area. Cairo, with a population of over 20 million, is one of the most densely populated cities in the world.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : POPULATION DISTRIBUTION IN WEST AFRICA

An ox-bow lake is a feature that develops from an old meander. As the meander wears out with age, a sand bar cuts across it. Thus trapping the head and creating a lake.

total population per annum		%

subject: Geography
q: Dairying is an agricultural practice that is most prevalent
explanation: Dairying is an agricultural practice that is most prevalent in extensive and undulating plains because of the lush pasture available for cattles.

subject: Geography
q: In river transport, the problem of irregular levels of water can be over come by
explanation: The problem of irregular levels of water in river transport can be overcome by building locks and canals. These help dam the river and artificially direct and store the course of water bodies.

subject: Geography
q: A population with low birth and death rates presents
explanation: A population with low birth and death rates presents an inverted population pyramid. This is because low birth rates mean fewer young people, and low death rates mean more older people. This results in a population structure that is wider at the top (older people) and narrower at the bottom (younger people), which is the shape of an inverted pyramid.

subject: Geography
q: The catalyst in the economic development of Japan is its
explanation: Japan's economic development is largely due to its large pool of skilled manpower. Japan has a highly educated and skilled workforce that has been instrumental in its economic development. The country is known for its technological advancements and industrial growth, which would not have been possible without a skilled workforce. The other options do not accurately represent the main catalyst in Japan's economic development.

subject: Geography
q: The pair of countries where conurbation is most highly developed is
explanation: The USA and England compared to other developed countries have the most profound conurbations where cities have expanded to engulf other suburbs, towns to form an urban continuum without any clear cut between towns any more.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following is most influential in the location of textile and footwear industries
explanation: Textile & Footwear industries use heavy weight raw materials as inputs for production hence raw materials heavily influence their location.

subject: Geography

explanation: Relaxation of currency control encourages massive imports as foreign exchange becomes readily available to transact foreign business deals.

making rural populations less homogeneous at the source region
creating job opportunities in the source areas
explanation: When rural migrants move to cities they engage in economic activities thus increasing the labour force and decreasing unemployment.

subject: Geography

explanation: Alternative energy guarantees consistent production as machines need to be powered by electricity to run.

subject: Geography

explanation: The Euracian Tundra is an ice riden landscape throughout the year, thus supporting a low population density.

explanation: Kenya's coastline is on the Indian Ocean blocked by the Rift-Valley system to the West. This encouraging subsidence of coastal airmasses.

options: the Mediterranean seaboard and the Guinea coast
explanation: Sugar plantations in Africa are most highly developed in the lowlands of Natal and the lower Nile. These regions have the ideal climate and soil conditions for sugar cane cultivation. Natal is a region in South Africa and the lower Nile refers to the region around the Nile river in Egypt.

explanation: Ferralitic soils in West Africa are mostly formed in the forest belt. They are highly weathered and low in nutrient reserves. Relying heavily on the cycle of forest tree leaves litter and organic matter for nutrient.

explanation: Kano ancient kingdom rose to early religious and economic significance both for pre - and during colonial times thus, supporting a large population.

subject: Geography
q: Some of the solid minerals found in Nigeria are
explanation: Nigeria is rich in solid minerals. Some of the solid minerals found in Nigeria include marble, tin, and columbite. Marble is used for construction and decorative purposes. Tin and columbite are used in various industries including the manufacturing of electronics.

subject: Geography
q: The Obudu Plateau of the cross River basin is economically noted for
explanation: The Obudu Plateau of the Cross River basin is economically noted for cattle ranching and tourism. The area is home to the Obudu Mountain Resort, which is a popular tourist destination. The cool climate of the plateau makes it suitable for cattle ranching.

explanation: Forest reservation which encourages maintaining forest land cover at water sheds (fringes of a river basin) help prevent degradation.

explanation: Tropical wet and dry(Air) Climate occurs when up to 10 months is wet with a single peak of rainfall in a year with 3000 mm annually and mean monthly temperature of ≥ 26oC.

subject: Geography
q: The survival of an organism in a natural ecosystem depends largely on
explanation: Charles Darwin proposed the theory of survival of the fittest. The survival of air organism in a natural ecosystem depends largely on its adaptation to prevailing conditions (resilience).

explanation: Lignite is an hydrocarbon substance similar to crudeoil. Though not fully decomposed like crude oil.

subject: Geography
q: The best technique to prevent soil in humid, gently sloping area is to adopt
explanation: Contour ridging is the best technique to prevent soil erosion in humid, gently sloping areas. This technique involves plowing across a slope following its elevation contour lines. These contour lines create a water break which reduces the formation of rills and gullies during times of heavy water run-off; which is a major cause of top soil loss and soil erosion.

explanation: Teak timber is found in the Equatorial forest. The eucalyptus is to the Australian woodland as the teak is to the Equatorial forest

subject: Geography
q: The existence of the Namibia Desert can be partly attributed to
explanation: The Namib Desert's existence can be partly attributed to the cold Benguela Current. The Benguela Current is a cold, northward flowing ocean current that forms part of the South Atlantic Ocean's broader circulation. It brings cold, low-salinity water from the Southern Ocean to the west coast of Southern Africa. This results in the arid conditions that characterize the Namib Desert.

subject: Geography

explanation:

∴ Longitudinal difference = 6 × 15° = 90°

subject: Geography
q: The two most important determinants of air temperature of a region are
explanation: Latitude & altitude are the most important determinants of air temperature of a region. The closer the latitude to Equator, the hotter the air masses & vice versa. Higher altitudes are associated with cooler air masses as air is forced to rise & condense.

explanation: Quartz feldspar & mica make up granitic rocks. They are crystalline in structure and hard with characteristic difference in colour.

explanation: Somalia & Western Australia have desert landscapes encouraged by local cold coastal currents running parallel to their coasts.

subject: Geography
q: The rock with the least carbon is
explanation: The rock with the least carbon is sandstone. Sandstone is a type of rock that is made up of sand-sized minerals or rock grains. Most sandstone is composed of quartz and/or feldspar because these are the most common minerals in the Earth's crust. It contains very little to no carbon, unlike coal and lignite which are carbon-rich.

subject: Geography
q: In a karst topography, the visible features on the landscape include
explanation: The sinkhole is one of the profound features in a Karst topography/limestone region as water dissolves joints at areas of least resistance to form spectacular depressions.

subject: Geography
q: A landform that may result mainly from wind erosion is a
explanation: Deflation hollow are cavities created by wind in the desert wadiis are formed by flash floods while barchan and seif dunes are made y wind deposition.

subject: Geography
q: In humid tropical regions, the major soil type is
explanation: Latosols are common in humid tropical regions rich in laterite (red soil) & covered by tropical vegetation.

subject: Geography
q: Bush burning affects the atmosphere directly by
explanation: Bush burning affects the gaseous part of the biosphere (the atmosphere) by adding CO2 accumulation.

subject: Geography
q: A process whereby a plant community is replaced by another is known as
explanation: Succession in ecology is a process whereby a plant community is replaced by another e.g mosses replaced by ferns.

subject: Geography
q: The most visible form of soil erosion is
explanation: Gully erosion is the most visible form of soil erosion. It occurs when runoff water accumulates and rapidly flows in narrow channels during or immediately after heavy rains or melting snow, removing the soil to a considerable depth. Gullies are typically deeper than 30cm. This makes it the most visible form of soil erosion.

subject: Geography
q: Softwood from coniferous forest is mainly used as raw material for the production of
explanation: Softwood from coniforest forests found in the coller regions close to the poles. North and south are best suited for the pulp and paper industry.

subject: Geography

options: topmost layer that may include organic litter
portion of the soil affected by leaching
zone of accumulation rich in bases and organic matter
explanation:
The a-layer is rich in organic litter
b - layer has decomposition and leaching occurring while C- layer is relatively unaffected by soil forming processes.

subject: Geography
q: One of the consequences of the polar icecaps melting is the
explanation: Polar ice caps melting is a result of global warming/climate change with/which is an increase in global mean temperature.

subject: Geography

explanation: The fertile/nutrient rich volcanic soil encouraged agric and hence population density in Jowak land of Indonesia.

subject: Geography
q: The most important characteristics of a population that enhance rapid economic development is the
explanation: The level of education influences to a large extent the rapid economic development of a population. As it is involved in several metrics key of such is Human Development Index.

subject: Geography

explanation: Rural population are (HDI) majorly agarian. Hence, quality of land for agric influences the pattern of rural population distribution.

subject: Geography

explanation: International migration entails population crossing a cross at least one international boundary between sovereign nations.

subject: Geography

explanation: Rural- Urban migration has been key in the rapid urban growth experienced in Sub-Saharan African cities.

subject: Geography

explanation: Rural settlements are usually made up of people from similar ancestral and cultural lineage/heritage, hence being homogenous.

subject: Geography

explanation: Iron & steel are major inputs for production by the developed countries of Europe, North America and Japan to produce industrial and home appliances and machines such as cars, ships etc

subject: Geography

explanation: When local raw materials are processed by manufacturing industries, the market prices of these products increase. Thus adding value to local products and contributing to national development via better GDP.

subject: Geography
q: Opencast mining often leads to the
explanation: Open Cast mining involves controlled/ uncontrolled blasting of the earth surface to extract mineral resources leading to degradation of the top soil.

subject: Geography
q: The reindeer and the camel are commonly used as beast of burden in the
explanation: Reindeers are beasts of burden adapted to ice conditions while camels are adapted to dusty desert conditions thus pro-polar and sahara desert respectively.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following groups of factors is most likely to influence industrial location?
explanation: Of all options provided the raw material, energy and market combination will most likely influence industrial location choice. Raw materials and energy are input factors while inlet output factor for ready purchase of product.

subject: Geography
q: Secondary economic activities are those that involve the
explanation:
Primary economic activities are
agro-based → Fishing, crop cultivation, animal rearing.
secondary → Production/processing of raw materials.
Tertiary → logistics, marketing.

subject: Geography
q: The towns that owe their origin and growth to the presence of localized mineral resources are
explanation: Towns that owe their origin and growth to the presence of localized mineral resources are often located near mines or other extraction sites. In Nigeria, Jos and Enugu are examples of such towns. Jos is known for its tin mining activities while Enugu is known for coal mining. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Jos and Enugu'.

subject: Geography
q: Soil conservation can be achieved through
explanation: Strip-cropping is crop cultivation is lines or steps which help in conserving soils especially along slopes & highland areas.

subject: Geography

explanation: The steel-rolling mill referred to in the question is located at Ovwian-Aladja in Delta State, Nigeria. This is a part of the Delta Steel Company, a subsidiary of the Nigerian Steel Development Authority.

explanation: The importance of wheat in Nigeria's agriculture has increased due to the introduction of large-scale irrigation. Irrigation has allowed for the cultivation of wheat in areas where it was previously not possible due to lack of sufficient rainfall. This has increased the production of wheat in the country.

explanation: Libya is one of the largest oil producers in Africa and the oil industry plays a significant role in its economy.

explanation: The Nile delta in Egypt, Volta in Ghana, Niger in Nigeria & Zambezi in Mozambique are all delta with magnificent features in Africa.

subject: Geography
q: The major factor responsible for the low population density in the west coast of Southern Africa is
explanation: The rugged relief (the Namib desert, cape of good hope, etc) is a major factor responsible for low population density in West coast of Southern Africa.

subject: Geography
q: The proportion of the earth's surface occupied by ocean is approximately
explanation: *Almost* 67% of the earth's surface is covered by oceans. Land mass accounts for about 33%.

subject: Geography
q: In the humid tropical lands, the structure of mature forest is closely related to the demand for
explanation: Sunlight influences the structure of mature forests in humid tropical lands. As it influences large shaped leaves to trap sunlight for photosynthesis, broad overlapping canopies at the top.

subject: Geography
q: Rocks which contain metals in concentrations high enough to be economically profitable to extract and refine are referred to as
explanation: Rocks which contain metals in concentrations high enough to be economically profitable to extract and refine are referred to as ores. An ore is a type of rock that contains sufficient minerals with important elements including metals that can be economically extracted from the rock. The ores are extracted through mining.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following groups consists of energy resources?
explanation: Coal, petroleum & water can all be used to generate energy based on commercial availability & affordability.

subject: Geography
q: Spruce, fir and pine are trees found in the
explanation: Spruce, fir, and pine are all types of trees that are typically found in coniferous forests. Coniferous forests are characterized by cone-bearing trees with needles rather than leaves. These types of trees are well adapted to survive in cold and temperate climates, which is where coniferous forests are typically found.

subject: Geography
q: Which of these hazards would most likely result directly from deforestation?
explanation: Other hazards stated are indirectly linked to deforestation. However, soil erosion is a direct result of deforestation as tree leaves & stem are no more available to low down & store rainwater while roots to slow down flood water & liquid oil are removed.

subject: Geography
q: The mass of air whose isobars form a circular shape, with low pressure in the centre and increasing towards the outside, is known as
explanation: The low pressure in the centre is referred to as the eye of the storm. This is a peculiar characteristics of hurricane.

subject: Geography
q: The process by which organic matter is decomposed and synthesized to form part of the soil is known as
explanation: Humification is the process by which organic matter is decomposed and synthesized to form part of the soil. It involves the transformation of biological material into a substance known as humus, a dark, stable form of organic matter that is resistant to further decomposition.

subject: Geography
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


What pattern of flow did the river develop towards the southern edge of the mapped area?
explanation: Braiding is the splitting of a water body into different lines as it flows down its river course-classically most spectacular forms of braiding are at river delta or at the source in impervious rock formation where water finds the path of least resistance to flow.

subject: Geography
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

In what stage of development is the river?
explanation: Old stage because lots of alluvial deposits are being observed, meaning low river velocity & high load volume of sediments with meander & braiding occurring.

subject: Geography
q: In an ecosystem, bacteria and some other microscopic organism are best described as
explanation: Bacteria & some other microscopic organisms form the 1st layer of action on decomposing organic matter or humification, thus referred to as primary decomposers. Shelf fungus is a fungus that grows on the sides of trees. It grows into the tree and decomposes it slowly.Two primary decomposers are fungi and bacteria. Their function in the recycling of life materials is very prominent because without them all organisms that died would decompose slowly.

subject: Geography
q: The process whereby water changes from liquid to gaseous state is called
explanation:
Evaporation is the process by which liquid changes to gaseous form
Freezing = Liquid to solid
Condensation = gaseous to liquid
Sublimation = Solid to gas

subject: Geography
q: A serious environmental hazard which causes both vertical and lateral displacement of parts of the earth's crust is
explanation: An earthquake is a sudden and violent shaking of the ground, often caused by movement of the Earth's tectonic plates. This movement can cause both vertical and lateral displacement of parts of the Earth's crust. Volcanoes, landslides, and weathering do not typically cause both types of displacement.

subject: Geography
q: If the standard time of a country is GMT + 1hr, which longitude's local time is adopted by the country?
explanation: Standard time is the local time at a country's standard meridian. This is usually based on the local time at a longitude that is a multiple of 15 degrees. If a country's standard time is GMT + 1 hour, it means that the country's standard meridian is 15 degrees to the east of the Prime Meridian (0 degrees longitude). Therefore, the correct answer is 15 degrees East.

subject: Geography
q: Migrant-receiving areas tend to be characterized by
explanation: Migrant-receiving areas are often characterized by extensive industrial development. This is because these areas often offer more job opportunities, which attract migrants. The other options, such as equal sex ratio, high per capita income, and the predominance of males, can vary depending on the specific circumstances of the migrant-receiving area.

subject: Geography
q: The settlement pattern associated with pastoral farming is
explanation: Pastoral farming like those found with fulani herdsmen are usually dispersed settlements. Having scattered individual families far apart in order to have adequate pasture for their herd of cattles.

subject: Geography
q: Air transportation is poorly developed in the Third World because of
explanation: A thriving economy will support all other options stated & thus eliminate these as negatives & boost the air transport industry in 3rd world countries.

subject: Geography
q: Squatter settlement are indicative of
explanation: When demand exceeds supply of decent housing with increasing low income settler migration, squatter settlements are bound to occur in urban centres.

subject: Geography
q: The invisible component of international trade consist of
explanation: Financial services & tourism are intangible components of high order international trade in services.

subject: Geography
q: A modern method of animal rearing in which pasture is grown for the animals is called
explanation: Ranching is the modern method of cattle rearing with special fodder grown for cattle on large expanse of land where cattles are lodged e.g Obudu Cattle Ranch.

subject: Geography
q: The human factor that contributes to the population growth of an area is
explanation: Industrialization is a human factor that contributes to the population growth of an area.

subject: Geography
q: Tourism is an example of
explanation: Tourism is an example of tertiary activities. Tertiary activities involve the provision of services to businesses as well as final consumers. Services may involve the transport, distribution and sale of goods from producer to a consumer, as in retailing, or may involve the provision of a service, such as in tourism.

subject: Geography

explanation: Areal expansion of town leads to development of suburbs at the urban fringes taking over agricultural lands.

explanation: Japan lacks natural resources and her industrialization is hinged on raw material import from all over the world.

explanation: More strict international collaborations to control trade in poached elephant tusks have yielded results globally.

subject: Geography

explanation: Due to increased population pressures in developing countries, desperate citizens coupled with harsh environmental condition have led to an increased trend in both legal & illegal international emigration.

explanation:

hut → homestead → hamlet → village.

explanation: Basement complex are old granitic rock which usually have developed smoothened sides overtime due to weathering & referred to as inselbergs.

subject: Geography
q: The most serious cause of desertification in Nigeria is
explanation: Desertification is the process by which fertile land becomes desert, typically as a result of inappropriate agricultural practices, deforestation, or climate change. In Nigeria, the most serious cause of desertification is excessive pressure on land. This is due to overgrazing, deforestation for fuelwood and inappropriate agricultural practices. These activities remove the vegetation cover of the soil, making it susceptible to erosion and eventually leading to desertification.

subject: Geography
q: The dominant foreign exchange earner of Senegal is
explanation: Fishing is the major foreign exchange earner for senegal with territorial coastal waters in the atlantic ocean. Unfortunately River senegal is completely surrounded by the Republic of Gambia.

subject: Geography
q: Recent growth in the manufacturing sector of the Nigerian economy is been mostly associated with the
options: growth of the iron and steel industry
explanation: With Nigeria's non-oil economy being dominated by agriculture, the diversification of this sector has reflected in local manufacturing sector growth in recent times.

subject: Geography
q: The Nigerian rivers that take their sources from the Jos plateau include
explanation: River Jamma' are flowing into Bauchi, Gongola into old Gongok State & Kaduna into Kaduna all have their origin from the Jos plateau.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : GEOGRAPHICAL REGIONS OF NIGERIA

q: Temperate and sub-tropical grasslands on the Africa continent are found in
explanation: South Africa & Lesotho. Both in the southern region of Africa with Lesotho. South Eastern region have temperate & Sub-Tropical grassland as a result of the shield created by the Drakensberg highlands to the coastal East.

subject: Geography
q: Equatorial trough or low-pressure belt is commonly known as the
explanation: Doldrums are equatorial trough or low-pressure belt. Cyclonic fronts are boundary lines generally are the tropics between high & low pressure air masses.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: The most common greenhouse gas in the atmosphere is
explanation: CO2 = Carbon(IV)oxide is the most dominant green house gas in the atmosphere because of human respiration coupled with rapid deforestation.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following sets of environmental resources is renewable?
explanation: Renewable resources are those that are replenished naturally and over relatively short periods of time. This includes resources like water, plants, and soils. Water is continuously cycled through the environment. Plants can regrow after harvesting and soils are formed over time from weathering of rocks and decay of organic material.

subject: Geography
q: The state of equilibrium reached by the vegetation of an area when it is left undisturbed for a long time is the
explanation: The state of equilibrium reached by the vegetation of an area when it is left undisturbed for a long time is known as the climax. In ecology, the climax community, or climatic climax community, is the state of equilibrium that a plant community will reach if undisturbed over time. It is the final stage of succession, remaining relatively unchanged until destroyed by an event such as fire or human interference.

subject: Geography
q: Rock minerals are said to be crystalline when the atoms forming them are
explanation: Rock minerals are said to be crystalline when the atoms forming them are arranged in a definite pattern. This is because the term 'crystalline' refers to a solid material whose constituents, such as atoms, molecules or ions, are arranged in a highly ordered, repeating pattern extending in all three spatial dimensions.

subject: Geography
q: A cool ocean current may bring to the coast it washes cool and
options: dry conditions if winds ar off shore
dry conditions if wind are on shore
wet conditions if winds are off shore
wet conditions if winds are on shore
explanation: Cool ocean currents are usually dry without moisture in nature & brings these conditions on shore/to continental land when flowing from the sea to the coast.

subject: Geography
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question



The rock that is igneous is
explanation: (H) Igneous rock are the basement rock upon which sedimentation occurs.

subject: Geography
q:

explanation: Limestone regions have the characteristic block-layered formation arranged over each other as found in M.

subject: Geography
q: Trees in the coniferous forest are noted for their
explanation: Coniferous forest of temperate regions characteristically have needle leaves & softwood. The softwood is what makes them good for pulp & paper industry.

subject: Geography
q: The difference in time between two longitudes is approximately ......
explanation:
1hr° = 15°
→ 60minutes = 15o
∴ x minutes = 1 °
x = 60/15 = 4minutes.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : THE EARTH AS A PLANET

subject: Geography
q: The concept of environmental balance can be taken to mean that an alteration in one of the components of the ecosystem may result in the
options: acceleration of the processes within the system
disruption of the processes within the system
minimization of the processes within the system
stabilization of the processes within the system
explanation: Alternation in a component of the ecosystem disrupts the process of succession towards equilibrium in an ecosystem.

subject: Geography
q: A small hill resulting from the process of peneplanation is called
explanation: Inselbergs are granite rocks worn down by population activities.

subject: Geography
q: Ocean currents flow in a clockwise direction in the northern hemisphere but in an anti-clockwise direction in the southern hemisphere because of the
options: rotation of the earth from east to west
strong and more persistent winds in the southern hemisphere
many active currents in the northern hemisphere
rotation of the earth from west to east.
explanation: Ferrel's law stating the role of coriolis effect/force acting on airmasses Northward deflected eastward as the earth rotates around its axis.

subject: Geography
q: A tropical soil formed on wind drift material and usually featuring a laterite layer is known as
explanation: Lithosols are savannah soils influenced by aeolian deposition in a vegetated surface. If this happens in a desert, then regosols are observed.

subject: Geography
q: A country which experiences earthquakes frequently is
explanation: Japan is located in the Pacific Ring of Fire, which is an area with a lot of seismic and volcanic activity. Therefore, Japan experiences earthquakes frequently. The Pacific Ring of Fire is a direct result of plate tectonics and the movement and collisions of lithospheric plates.

subject: Geography
q: Plants are of primary importance to animals within an ecosystem because they
options: can be composted and provide nutrient to soils
produce oxygen and provide sinks for carbon(IV)oxide
explanation: Man breathes in O2 & out CO2 while plants use CO2 for photosynthesis & release O2 into the atmosphere for animal respiration.

subject: Geography
q: The most critical and variable element of weather in a tropical environment is
explanation: The most critical and variable element of weather in a tropical environment is rainfall. This is because rainfall in tropical environments is highly variable and unpredictable, and it has a significant impact on the ecosystem, including the types of plants and animals that can survive there. While temperature, sunshine hours, and potential evaporation are also important elements of weather, they are generally less variable in a tropical environment than rainfall.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: Urban and rural settlements are best differentiated by their
explanation: Population = number of people & Function = kind of economic activities carried out are major determinants in differentiating urban from rural settlements.

subject: Geography
q: The most dense airline network in the world is found in
explanation: The most dense airline network in the world is found in Western Europe and North America. This is due to the high level of economic development, large population, and high demand for air travel in these regions.

subject: Geography
q: Harsh desert conditions are responsible for the low population density in the
explanation: Harsh desert conditions are responsible for the low population density in the west coast of South Africa. This region is known as the Namib Desert, which is one of the oldest and driest regions in the world, making it difficult for human habitation.

subject: Geography
q: The joining together of many cities results in the formation of a
explanation: The joining together of many cities results in the formation of a conurbation. A conurbation is a region comprising a number of cities, large towns, and other urban areas that, through population growth and physical expansion, have merged to form one continuous urban and industrially developed area.

subject: Geography
q: The raw material for terylene is
explanation: Terylene is a type of polyester that is made from petroleum products. Petroleum is processed to produce ethylene glycol and terephthalic acid, which are then combined to form terylene.

subject: Geography
q: The most important effect of migration on population is
explanation: Migration leads to redistribution of population. It involves the movement of people from one place to another which results in the change of population distribution.

subject: Geography
q: The Ruhr coal field is located in
explanation: The Ruhr coal field is located in Germany. It is one of the largest industrial regions in Europe and was once the largest coal-mining area in the world.

subject: Geography
q: British foreign investment are concentrated in
explanation: The Commonwealth countries are all part of British colonized/Independent countries with heavy British investments & economic interests.

subject: Geography
q: South-facing slopes are favourable for rural settlement locations in the cool temperate regions of the northern hemisphere because they
explanation: South-facing slopes also referred to as Advet support rural settlements. In cool temperate regions, a crop cultivation is made possible & less cold experienced due to its slope & angle inclination, allowing for Solar radiation from the Equator to hit this area as against North-facing slopes which are shielded from solar radiation from the Equator.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following features affects intervisibility between two settlements?
explanation: Intervisibility between two settlements is affected by a high hill. Intervisibility refers to the ability to see from one place to another. A high hill can obstruct the line of sight between two settlements, thereby affecting their intervisibility. Other features like deep trenches, broad valleys, and wide rivers may also affect intervisibility, but not to the same extent as a high hill.

subject: Geography
q: One operation common to all survey methods is
explanation: Reconnaissance is the process of carrying out initial field observation to observe the terrain of the area to be surveyed & confirm earlier sketched features to reality. This helps the team plan for the actual surveying exercise.

subject: Geography
q: The method used for coal mining in Nigeria is
explanation: The method used for coal mining in Nigeria is open cast. Open cast mining is a surface mining technique of extracting rock or minerals from the earth by their removal from an open-air pit, sometimes known as a borrow. This form of mining differs from extractive methods that require tunnelling into the earth, such as long wall mining.

explanation: Ozone layer depletion is a direct effect of greenhouse gases on the stratosphere/Tropopause zone. However, global warming directly affects the troposphere leading to negative impacts of climate change as shown in coastlands drowning & increased sea surges.

explanation: The lithosphere is the solid/soil/rock component of the Troposphere. Most of its materials are made from Silica, Iron & Alluminium. explanation: Nitrogen fixing organisms whether flora or fauna help in enriching the soil with Nitrogen, a macronutrient, important for plant growth.

subject: Geography
q: The two most important factors influencing the evolution of a climax vegetation are
explanation: In an ecological succession, soil & time play a vital role for vegetation to reach climax. Soil development from nutrient enriched water bodies to solid ground through causally cyclic growth → death → decomposition of lower order plants is key. Also time has to be allowed for each stage of the ecological succession process without interruption.

explanation: The instrument used for measuring atmospheric pressure is a barometer. Atmospheric pressure is the force exerted by the weight of the air in the atmosphere of Earth. explanation: In the broadest sense, the two major types of ecosystems are terrestrial and aquatic. Terrestrial ecosystems are land-based, while aquatic ecosystems are water-based. The other options do not correctly categorize the major types of ecosystems.

subject: Geography

explanation: Sandstone is a type of rock whose principal constituents can be seen without magnification. It is a clastic sedimentary rock composed mainly of sand-sized mineral particles or rock fragments. The other options are not typically visible without magnification.

explanation: Monocropping interfers with the normal proces of ecological succession making the flora more susceptible to that particular species pests and diseases. explanation: Particle sizes determine the texture of a soil. Smaller sizes are finer e.g clay while larger sizes are coarse e.g sand.

explanation: Differentials in salinity between fresh and salt water bodies (inland rivers versus oceans) as well as temperature variation between land and sea influence the cycles of ocean currents globally.

explanation: A pie chart shows information in degrees. Each slice of the pie represents a category of data. The size of the slice is proportional to the quantity it represents in the dataset. explanation: Ocean currents only have effects upon adjoining coasts when they make land falls.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following is a warm current?
explanation: The East Australian Current is a warm ocean current that moves water from the Coral Sea down the east coast of Australia. Warm currents are those that flow from low latitudes into high latitudes. They are typically found on the east side of continents in the tropics and subtropics.

subject: Geography
q: The speed of wind is usually measured using
explanation: An anemometer is a device used for measuring the speed of wind, and is also a common weather station instrument. The term is derived from the Greek word anemos, which means wind, and is used to describe any wind speed instrument used in meteorology.

explanation: Mediterranean climate has dry summer and wet winter. Also known as the Dry summer subtropical climate with hot dry summers & mild wet winters.

subject: Geography
q: One distinguishing characteristics of ferruginous soils is the
options: occurrence of iron concretion in the B-horizon
high content of organic matter in all the horizons
explanation: The iron and aluminium form distinct layer below the layer of humus/top soil. This having well-developed A & B horizons.

subject: Geography
q: The main mineral composition of lateritic soils are
explanation: Iron and aluminium are the main mineral composition of lateritic soils giving it its characteristic red colour.

subject: Geography
q: A mineral that is essential in cement production is
explanation: Gypsum is an indispensible component of cement. It is added to control the rate of hardening of cement. It is a soft white or gray mineral made up of integrated calcium sulphate.

subject: Geography
q: Shifting cultivation in agriculture involves
options: intensive cultivation in a highly populated region
cultivating alternative plots to allow natural regeneration
explanation: Shifting cultivation is an old traditional practise that entails crop cultivation on alternate plots to allow for natural regeneration.

subject: Geography
q: One direct adverse environmental effect of deforestation is
explanation: One direct adverse environmental effect of deforestation is escalated soil erosion. When trees are cut down, the soil is left exposed and can be easily washed away by rain. This leads to soil erosion, which can degrade the land and make it less productive for agriculture.

subject: Geography
q: A process that creates deep gorges on the land surface is
explanation: Sheet erosion is a process of erosion that removes a uniform layer of soil from the surface of an area. This process can lead to the formation of deep gorges on the land surface. The other options do not directly relate to physical geographical processes that shape the land surface.

subject: Geography
q: The most sparsely populated areas of the world include
explanation: Canada and Eurasia are among the most sparsely populated areas of the world. This is due to their harsh climates and vast, uninhabitable landscapes. The other options, such as China and Japan, are among the most densely populated areas of the world.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following river basins is most densely populated?
explanation: The gauges cutting through Nepal/India region have supported a large population density from early times because of the fertile alluvial soil for paddy rice farming.

subject: Geography
q: The movement of people from a village to an irrigation project area may be described as
explanation: The movement of people from a village to an irrigation project area is described as rural-rural migration. This is because both the village and the irrigation project area are in rural settings. Urban-urban migration refers to the movement of people within urban areas. Rural-urban migration refers to the movement of people from rural areas to urban areas. Urban-rural migration refers to the movement of people from urban areas to rural areas.

subject: Geography
q: Access to water and kinship ties can lead to
explanation: Loose-knot settlements meet the criteria for both access to kinship ties (nucleated) and water (ribbon/linear). As such its a merger of both components.

subject: Geography
q: Sydney, Hong Kong and San Francisco have attained considerable importance because of their function as
explanation: Sydney, Hong kong & San Francisco are renowned for their high traffic seaports in their respective regions.

subject: Geography
q: A country that has its railways operating on three different gauges is
explanation: Australia runs on all broad, narrow and standard railway gauges within its territorial boundaries.

subject: Geography
q: The three leading manufacturing nations of the world are
explanation: Modern day china, USA & Germany are the 3 leading manufacturing countries of the world.

subject: Geography
q: Industrial location can be influenced by
explanation: Raw materials & energy (input) and market (sales) to a large extent determine or influence location of industry.

subject: Geography
q: Commercial agriculture in the tropics concentrates on the production of
explanation: The tropics are characterized by hot and humid weather and abundant rainfall. Crops like tea, coffee, sugar cane, bananas, mangoes, coconut, and spices are commonly grown in these regions.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following Nigerian towns is likely to experience frequent cases of coastal erosion?
explanation: Burutu is a town and Local Government Area (LGA) in Delta State, Nigeria. It is a river port on the Forcados River, one of the Niger Delta waterways. Therefore, it is more likely to experience coastal erosion.

explanation: Niger state is home to kainji and shiroro dams reputable for Hydro-Electric Power generation in Nigeria.

subject: Geography
q: Zuma rock, Zaria and Kano isenberg's in Nigeria are within the confines of the
explanation: Zuma rock, Zaria and Kano isenberg's in Nigeria are within the confines of the North central highlands. The North central highlands is a geographical region in Nigeria that includes the Federal Capital Territory and some states in the North Central geopolitical zone of Nigeria. This region is characterized by a highland area with numerous rocks and hills.

subject: Geography
q: The Nigerian river with the longest distance of internal waterways is
explanation: The Nigerian river with the longest distance of internal waterways is River Benue. River Benue is the major tributary of the Niger River, which is the principal river of western Africa. It flows approximately 1,400 kilometers from its source in the Adamawa Plateau to its confluence with the Niger River.

subject: Geography
q: Trees in the mangroove forest have aerial roots because they
explanation: Due to the salinity of swamp/ocean water, mangrove forests have aerial roots to absorb water from the atmosphere.

subject: Geography
q: A settlement that derives its domestic water from a waterfall in Nigeria is
explanation: Erin-Ijesa in Osun State, Nigeria is known for its waterfall, the Erin-Ijesa Waterfall, also known as Olumirin Waterfall. Therefore, it's likely that the settlement derives its domestic water from this waterfall.

subject: Geography
q: Wheat is cultivated through irrigation in Nigeria in
explanation: Nasarawa state has irrigation farming to cultivate wheat.

explanation: Though sunlight is renewable, hydro-electricity outplays it on the measure of being economically affordable in the short term.

subject: Geography
q: The equatorial climate is found in the
explanation: Zaire Basin of Central Africa (DR congo and Republic of Congo) and adjoining areas is home to the Equatorial climate and associated Equatorial rainforest vegetation.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : AFRICA ON BROAD OUTLINE

subject: Geography
q: The arid regions of Africa have great potentials for
explanation: Solar energy/radiation is abundant in the arid regions of Africa. From Sahara to Namib to kalahari etc, the volume of solar energy due to absence of cloud cover is tremendous.

explanation: All other answers/options can be addressed (large youthful population, high population of unskilled and skilled labour) when industrialization is increased at a fast pace to reduce high unemployment levels in West Africa.

explanation: Most economic activities in Tropical Africa still revolve around Primary Production (agro-allied and agriculture) related activities.

subject: Geography
q: Khartoum in Sudan is located
explanation: Khartoum, the capital of Sudan, is located at the confluence of the White Nile and the Blue Nile. This is where the two rivers merge to form the Nile River, which flows northward through Egypt to the Mediterranean Sea. A confluence is a location where two or more bodies of water meet.

is land-based while communication is air - borne

subject: Geography
q: A hard, shiny and carbon-rich organic rock that burns smokelessly is
explanation: Anthracite is a hard, shiny, and carbon-rich organic rock that burns smokelessly. It is the highest rank of coal and contains the highest carbon content, between 86% and 98%. This makes it the cleanest burning coal, and it gives off a lot of heat when it burns. Peat, rhyolite, and marble are not carbon-rich organic rocks that burn smokelessly.

explanation: Limestone and clay metamorphosis under adequate pressure and heat overtone to give marble and slate.

subject: Geography
q: The portion of the land which rises higher than other areas is described as
explanation: A block mountain or a horst is a portion of the land that rises higher than other areas due to geological activities. It is formed by the movement of large crustal blocks when forces in the Earth's crust cause it to break apart. It is not necessarily a volcanic cone, an anticline hill, or an escarpment. Therefore, the correct answer is 'a block mountain'.

subject: Geography
q: A seif is a landform associated with
explanation: Seif dunes in deserts results from dolian/wind deposition of sand to form spectacular ridge-like features.

explanation: Plugdome: is a characteristic feature of an extinct basaltic volcanic region where magma has cooled down the major vent of the volcano to form a dome.

subject: Geography

explanation: The atlantic ocean is the largest ocean in the world bordering Europe, Africa, North and South America and the caribbeans.
