q: The classification of clouds is based on a combination of form, height and
explanation: Having classified clouds by form and height the last major distinguishing feature of note is the appearance fluffy, plumes etc.

subject: Geography
q: Hygrometer is used to measure
explanation: A hygrometer is an instrument used to measure the amount of humidity and water vapor in the atmosphere, in soil, or in confined spaces. Humidity measurement in meteorology is of great importance as it affects both weather and climate. Therefore, the correct answer is 'relative humidity'.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: The temperature at which condensation occurs when air is saturated is the
explanation: The temperature at which the water vapor in the air becomes saturated and condensation begins is called the dew point. It is the temperature at which air becomes saturated, causing the formation of dew droplets. Therefore, the correct answer is 'dew point'.

explanation: Spruce, for and pines are softwood trees found in temperate coniferous forests used in pulp & paper industry.

explanation: Wet & dry seasons encourage laterization (formation of laterite) in humid tropics. This is aided by the peculiar vegetation cover formed here a source of organic matter.

subject: Geography

explanation: Intrazonal soils have a well defined soil profile characteristics reflecting the dominant influence of some relier factors or parent material.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following sources of power supply is renewable?
explanation: Solar power is a renewable source of energy. It is derived from the sun's radiation and can be converted into electricity or heat. Unlike fossil fuels such as coal and gas, solar power does not deplete natural resources or cause environmental pollution.

subject: Geography
q: The most critical element in an ecosystem is the
options: rate of growth of species within the system
explanation: Energy flow determines survival, nutrient & energy transfer from one trophic level to the other as well as primary food production by micro-organisms and plants.

subject: Geography
q: Flooding in coastal areas can best be checked by
explanation: Channelization is the artificial method of forcing water to flow within a restricted pathway constructed.

subject: Geography
q: The major geographical factors that determine the pattern of population distribution include
explanation: Agriculture, soil, climate and minerals have since the origin of man till date been major influences on settlement formation as well as pattern development & population growth.

subject: Geography
q: The population pyramid for a developed country has a narrow base which indicates
explanation: A population pyramid for a developed country typically has a narrow base, which indicates a low birth rate. This is because developed countries often have lower fertility rates due to factors such as better access to contraception, higher levels of education, and the high cost of raising children.

subject: Geography
q: Detroit in the U.S.A. came into prominence as a result of its
explanation: Detroit in the U.S.A. came into prominence as a result of its automobile industries. It is famously known as the birthplace of the American automobile industry and was once home to the headquarters of General Motors, Ford, and Chrysler.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following explains the importance of the concept of site and situation of settlements?
options: Both site and situation are most favourable for the establishment and growth of settlements.
All site and situations are located on plains
Both site and situation are not favourable to the establishment and growth of settlements
All settlements are located on site and situation
explanation: For a settlement to be founded at a particular location, then, its site and situation must favour and be able to support a settlement as of that time.

subject: Geography
q: The world's busiest seaport is
explanation: The world's busiest seaport is Rotterdam. This is based on the volume of cargo handled. Rotterdam is strategically located and has excellent infrastructure, making it a major hub for international trade.

subject: Geography

explanation: Economic diversification is a direct impact of industrialization for developing countries as activities more from primary to secondary & tertiary economic activities dominating EDP.

subject: Geography

explanation: Sudan savanna in Nigeria is semi-desert in nature with little rainfall sparsely over few months in a year.

explanation: Yobe state in Nigeria is most vulnerable to drought and desertification. This is due to its location in the Sahel region, which is characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures. The other states listed are not as vulnerable to drought and desertification as Yobe.

explanation: Iron & Steel are the foundation for industrialization as most factory's machineries & plants are made with them.

explanation: Ladi Kwali was a famous Nigerian potter who was known for her skills in pottery. She was awarded the Nigerian National Merit Award for her contributions to the development of pottery in Nigeria. Therefore, the craft industry associated with Ladi Kwali in Nigeria is pottery.

subject: Geography
q: An area in Shaba province in the Democratic Republic of Congo known for the production of copper is
explanation: Lubumbashi is a city in the southeastern part of the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is known for its copper production. It is located in the Shaba (now Katanga) province, which is rich in mineral resources, especially copper.

explanation: Mauritania is a desert country in North-Western Africa lacking rivers dropping overheight a scenarios for HEP exploitation.

explanation: Lake Volta is an example of a man-made lake. It was created by the construction of the Akosombo Dam on the Volta River in Ghana. Man-made lakes, also known as reservoirs, are usually formed by damming rivers to store water for various uses such as hydroelectric power generation, irrigation, and water supply.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following systems provides both irrigation water and hydro-electricity?
explanation: The Aswan High Dam in Egypt provides both irrigation water and hydro-electricity. The dam controls the flow of the Nile River, providing water for irrigation and generating hydroelectric power.

explanation: Cape Town in South Africa has similar characteristics to the Mediterranean Climatic zone also found in North Africa region from Morocco to Tunisia coastal lands.

explanation: Coal is a black or brownish-black sedimentary rock that is primarily composed of carbon, along with various other elements such as hydrogen, sulfur, oxygen, and nitrogen. Therefore, the main composition of coal is carbon.

Hence, it is a prominent erosional landform of wind action.

= 13oC

then at 2000m above sea level - 13oC - 10oC = -3oC.

Component of the soil includes inorganic matter, organic matter, soil water, soil air and living organism

explanation: Land ecosystem includes vegetation's, soil lithosphere and oceans. These encompasses both flora/fauna and their natural habitats.

explanation: Global warming is an increase in mean temperature globally thus melting ice-caps of mountains and snow/icebergs at the poles leading to rise in the volume of ocean waters.

explanation: Migration primarily affects the distribution of the population. People move from one place to another, which leads to a redistribution of the population. Therefore, the correct answer is option 2, 'redistribution of the population'.

explanation: Site is the actual physical place a settlement occupies. Situation is the surrounding physical and anthropological circumstances around which the settlement is located (neighbouring descriptors).

subject: Geography
q: A map with a scale of 1:40,000 is reduced to one quarter of its size, what is the new scale?
explanation: When a map is reduced to one quarter of its size, the scale is quadrupled. This is because the scale of a map is the ratio of a distance on the map to the corresponding distance on the ground. Therefore, if the map is reduced to one quarter of its size, the distance on the map is now one quarter of what it was, but the corresponding distance on the ground remains the same. Therefore, the new scale is 1:40,000 x 4 = 1:160,000.

subject: Geography
q: An example of a great circle is
explanation: A great circle is the largest circle that can be drawn on a sphere. It is the intersection of the sphere with a plane that passes through the center of the sphere. On the Earth, the equator is an example of a great circle. Other examples of great circles include all meridians of longitude, which intersect the North and South Poles, and the equator. Latitudes other than the equator are not great circles, they are smaller circles.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : THE EARTH AS A PLANET

subject: Geography
q: A metamorphosed form of shale is
explanation: Schist is a medium-grade metamorphic rock with medium to large, flat, sheet-like grains in a preferred orientation (nearby grains are roughly parallel).

subject: Geography
q: Sandstone can be classified as
explanation: Sandstone is a type of sedimentary rock that is primarily composed of sand-sized mineral particles or rock fragments, hence it is classified as arenaceous. The term 'arenaceous' is derived from the Latin word 'arena' which means sand.

subject: Geography
q: A Karst feature in its advanced stage of development is
explanation: Karst topography is a landscape formed from the dissolution of soluble rocks such as limestone, dolomite, and gypsum. It is characterized by underground drainage systems with sinkholes, dolines, and caves and then at advanced stages,Karst valley.

subject: Geography
q: The common features of the middle course of a river are
explanation: They are major features of the middle course. The common features of the middle course of a river are interlocking spurs and meanders.

subject: Geography
q: A form of chemical weathering is
explanation: Carbonation is a form of chemical weathering. It occurs when carbon dioxide in the air or water reacts with minerals in rocks, causing them to dissolve. The other options listed, fragmentation, exfoliation, and shattering, are all forms of physical weathering, which involves the breakdown of rocks without any change in their chemical composition.

subject: Geography
q: Ocean currents affect the coast they wash when
explanation: As they blow over the ocean, they either carry water or not depending on whether they are cold or warm current.

subject: Geography
q: Wind vane is used to record the
explanation: A weather vane, wind vane, or weathercock is an instrument for showing the direction of the wind. They are typically used as an architectural ornament to the highest point of a building.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: What is the climate of a station with an annual rainfall of 2650mm spread fairly all year round and a mean annual temperature of 27oC?
explanation: The climate described in the question is a Tropical humid climate. This type of climate is characterized by high temperatures (mean annual temperature of 27 degrees Celsius) and high rainfall (2650mm) spread fairly all year round. Tropical wet and dry climate has a distinct dry season, which is not mentioned in the question. Tropical monsoon climate has a very heavy but short rainy season. Mediterranean woodland climate has hot, dry summers and mild, wet winters, which is not the case here.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following types of vegetation is characterized by acidic soils?
explanation: High solar radiation & evaporation as the soil is often exposed and this increases acidic content.

subject: Geography
q: When both the macropores and the micropores are filled with water, the soil is
explanation: When both the macropores and the micropores are filled with water, the soil is said to be waterlogged. Macropores and micropores are spaces in the soil. When these spaces are filled with water, it prevents air from getting to the roots of plants, causing the soil to be waterlogged.

subject: Geography
q: A mature soil with distinct horizons occurring in a particular climatic zone, but influenced strongly by topography is
explanation: Intrazonal soils are mature soils with distinct horizons occurring in a particular climatic zone, but influenced strongly by topography. They are soils that have developed under conditions of a particular climate, but their formation is strongly influenced by local factors such as topography.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following resources is non-renewable?
explanation: Nuclear energy is a non-renewable resource. It is produced from uranium, a non-renewable resource that exists in limited quantities in the Earth's crust. Once these reserves are depleted, they cannot be replenished.

subject: Geography
q: The introduction of materials that have negative impacts on the ecosystem is known as
explanation: The introduction of materials that have negative impacts on the ecosystem is known as pollution. Pollution refers to the introduction of harmful materials into the environment. These harmful materials are called pollutants. Pollutants can be natural, such as volcanic ash, or created by human activity, such as trash or runoff produced by factories. Pollutants damage the quality of air, water, and land.

subject: Geography
q: An example of a climatic hazard is
explanation: A climatic hazard is a type of disaster that is caused by weather or climate. This includes hurricanes, tornadoes, blizzards, and droughts. A hurricane is a type of storm that occurs in tropical or subtropical areas, typically in the summer or fall. Hurricanes are characterized by their strong, spiraling winds and heavy rainfall. They can cause significant damage to buildings and infrastructure, as well as loss of life.

subject: Geography
q: In which of the following regions is high population density attributable to agricultural development?
explanation: High population density is often attributable to agricultural development, as agriculture provides food and employment opportunities, which attract people to an area. The Nile Basin is a prime example of this. The Nile River provides a fertile valley in the middle of the desert, which has allowed for intensive agriculture for thousands of years. This has led to a high population density in the region.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following statements best describes the population of Southeast Asia?
explanation: Southeast Asia is known for its high population density due to the concentration of people in urban areas and fertile plains. It also has a high growth rate due to high birth rates and declining death rates. This is due to factors such as improved healthcare and living conditions.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following population characteristics is associated with developed countries?
options: Declining birth rate and high death rate
High birth rate and declining death rate
High birth rate and low death rate
Low birth rate and low death rate
explanation: Developed countries are characterized by low birth rates and low death rates. This is due to factors such as better healthcare, education, and access to contraception, which lead to smaller family sizes and longer life expectancy.

subject: Geography
q: Urban settlements mainly engage in
explanation: It includes both secondary and tertiary activities.

subject: Geography
q: Lagos, Sydney, Singapore, Hong Kong and Calabar are cities which have attained considerable importance because they
explanation: Lagos, Sydney, Singapore, Hong Kong and Calabar are cities that have attained considerable importance because they have seaports. Seaports are crucial for international trade as they facilitate the import and export of goods. This makes cities with seaports significant hubs of economic activity.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following arranges the settlement types in descending order in terms of complexity of functions?
explanation: The correct arrangement of settlement types in descending order in terms of complexity of functions is Metropolis, city, town, village and hamlet. A metropolis is a large city, often the most important city in a large area or country, with many people and activities. A city is a large or important town, while a town is a built-up area with a name, defined boundaries, and local government, that is larger than a village and generally smaller than a city. A village is a group of houses and associated buildings, larger than a hamlet and usually smaller than a town, and sometimes (as in parts of the U.S.) incorporated as a municipality. Lastly, a hamlet is a small settlement, generally one smaller than a village, and strictly (in Britain) one without a church.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following economic activities are classified as primary?
explanation: Primary economic activities are those that involve the extraction of resources directly from the Earth. This includes activities such as fishing, lumbering, farming, and mining. These activities are considered primary because they involve the production of raw materials that can be transferred into a final product. Hence, the correct answer is 'Fishing, lumbering, farming and mining'.

subject: Geography
q: When a manufacturing enterprise produces goods which form raw materials for other enterprises, it is referred to as
explanation: It is the process of entering an agreement to perform certain work to complement the production of another company.

subject: Geography
q: Most less developed nations are trade debtors because
options: of their continuous link with colonial masters
of the gross inbalance in international trade
they import manufactured goods from industrialized nations
explanation: Their economy produces raw materials but imports most of the finished goods.

subject: Geography
q: Large-scale wheat production is associated with the
options: Ghezira Plains and the Pampas of Argentina
Prairies of North America and the Pampas of Argentina
Ganges Plains and the Pampas of Argentina
explanation: Large-scale wheat production is associated with the Prairies of North America and the Pampas of Argentina. These regions have the ideal climate and soil conditions for wheat cultivation, and they are known for their extensive wheat fields.

subject: Geography
q: Liberia, Malaysia and Indonesia are known for the production of
explanation: Liberia, Malaysia and Indonesia are known for the production of rubber. These countries have tropical climates which are suitable for the growth of rubber trees. Rubber is a major export product for these countries.

subject: Geography
q: The contribution of agriculture to Nigeria's GDP has remained largely low owing to
explanation: The contribution of agriculture to Nigeria's GDP has remained largely low due to its predominantly subsistence nature. Subsistence agriculture is a type of farming in which nearly all of the crops or livestock raised are used to maintain the farmer and the farmer's family, leaving little, if any, surplus for sale or trade. This means that the output is generally low and not enough to significantly contribute to the GDP.

q: Nguru and Kaura Namoda settlements in Northern Nigeria are most notable for

explanation: Nguru and Kaura Namoda settlements in Northern Nigeria are most notable for being terminal points for railway transportation. The railway system in Nigeria was a major means of transportation during the period in question, and these settlements served as terminal points, making them notable in this regard.

subject: Geography
q: The scattered settlement pattern of the Tiv of the Middle Belt is owing to the
explanation: The scattered settlement pattern of the Tiv of the Middle Belt is owing to the agricultural practices of the region. The Tiv people are predominantly farmers, and their settlement pattern is influenced by their farming activities. They tend to settle in scattered communities close to their farmlands, leading to a scattered settlement pattern.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following African countries is landlocked?
explanation: Zambia is a landlocked country in Africa. A landlocked country is a country that does not have a coastline. It is surrounded by land on all sides. Gabon, Sudan, and Mozambique all have coastlines.

subject: Geography
q: The relief system in East Africa is characterized by
explanation: The relief system in East Africa is characterized by rift valleys. A rift valley is a linear shaped lowland between several highlands or mountain ranges created by the action of a geologic rift or fault. The East African Rift system is an example of where this occurs.

q: The length between two points on a topographical map is 5cm. What is the actual distance between them if the scale of the map is 1:50000?
explanation: The scale of the map is 1:50000, which means 1cm on the map represents 50000cm (or 0.5km) in reality. Therefore, if the length between two points on the map is 5cm, the actual distance between them would be 5cm * 0.5km/cm = 2.5km. Hence, option 1 is correct.

explanation: Chain survey is a method of surveying in which only linear measurements are made in the field. It is most convenient for measurement on flat or plain terrain where there are fewer obstacles. Hence, option 2 is correct.

subject: Geography
q: The earth's crust consists essentially of
explanation: The earth's crust is primarily composed of rocks which are made up of minerals. The most abundant minerals in the earth's crust are Feldspar and Quartz, which are made up of Silica and Aluminium. Hence, option 2 is correct.

subject: Geography
q: The rock type formed through the deposits compression of elastic particles is
explanation: Mudstone is an extremely fine-grained sedimentary rock consisting of a mixture of clay and silt-sized particles.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following rock types is formed through the ejection of magma onto the surface of the earth?
explanation: Basalt is a type of rock that is formed through the ejection of magma onto the surface of the earth. When magma reaches the earth's surface and cools quickly, it forms basalt. The other options, lignite, granite, and slate, are formed through different geological processes.

subject: Geography
q: Rocks that are composed of particles derived from previously existing rocks are referred to as
explanation: Mechanically formed sedimentary rocks are formed from the remains of either already established rocks.

q:

explanation: It is a shallow water zone that is close to and surrounds the continent.

subject: Geography
q: An ox-bow lake is usually located in a
explanation: An ox-bow lake is a landform by river at the lower course.

subject: Geography
q: The phenomenon of change in direction of wave fronts as they approach the sea shore is known as
explanation: Wave refraction is the process by which the direction of waves travel is changed as they pass from one medium to another, or when they pass across areas of different depth. As waves approach the shore, they are often refracted, or bent, and this is what the question refers to. Therefore, the correct answer is 'wave refraction'.

subject: Geography
q: The storm which occurs over the China Sea between latitudes 8o and 15oN and S of the equator is a
explanation: The storm which occurs over the China Sea between latitudes 8 degrees and 15 degrees N and S of the equator is a typhoon. A typhoon is a mature tropical cyclone that develops in the western part of the North Pacific Ocean between 180° and 100°E. This region is referred to as the Northwestern Pacific Basin, and is the most active tropical cyclone basin on Earth, accounting for almost one-third of the world's annual tropical cyclones. Therefore, the correct answer is 'typhoon'.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: A major characteristic of the tropical continental climate is the
options: occurrence of high diurnal temperatures throughout the year
absence of cloud cover throughout the year
prevalence of high and constant temperatures throughout the year
occurrence of alternating wet and dry seasons throughout the year
explanation: The tropical continental climate is characterized by the occurrence of alternating wet and dry seasons throughout the year. This is due to the movement of the Inter-Tropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ) which brings rainfall during its presence and dry conditions when it moves away.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: Plants that are highly tolerant of drought and can survive in dry habitats are referred to as
explanation: Xerophtytes are drought-resistant plants. They are plants which are adapted to dry/desert areas. To survive these harsh conditions they have special features. For example, a cactus has white hairs which help to prevent water loss. Another example is Manzanita plants, which have a thick waxy coating and keep their leaves vertical to the sun.

subject: Geography
q: An important characteristic of the B - horizon in tropical ferruginous soils is the
explanation: The B-horizon in tropical ferruginous soils is characterized by the accumulation of oxides. This is due to the process of leaching, where water carries soluble substances or small particles through the soil or other material. In the case of tropical ferruginous soils, iron and aluminium oxides are leached from the upper layers and accumulate in the B-horizon.

subject: Geography
q: The texture of the soil depends on the constituent of
explanation: The texture of the soil is determined by the relative proportions of sand, silt, and clay it contains. This is known as the soil's mineral composition. Sand particles are the largest and tend to have good drainage but low water-holding capacity. Clay particles are the smallest and tend to have poor drainage but high water-holding capacity. Silt particles are intermediate in size and have properties in between sand and clay.

subject: Geography
q: The most significant economic use of clean water is for
explanation: While all the options listed are uses of clean water, the most significant economic use is for drinking. This is because clean drinking water is a basic necessity for human survival and health. Without it, people cannot survive, and the cost of treating water-related diseases can be high. Therefore, ensuring a supply of clean drinking water is a top priority for any economy.

subject: Geography
q: The major terrestrial ecosystems are often referred to as
explanation: The major terrestrial ecosystems are often referred to as biomes. A biome is a large community of plants and animals that occupies a distinct region. Biomes are defined by factors such as plant structures (such as trees, shrubs, and grasses), leaf types (such as broadleaf and needleleaf), plant spacing (forest, woodland, savanna), and climate. Unlike 'biomass', which refers to the total mass of organisms in a given area or volume, 'grasslands' and 'boreal forests' are examples of specific types of biomes.

subject: Geography
q: Global warming directly result in
options: melting ice cap and rising sea level
explanation: It increases temperature and causes ice to melt, especially in polar regions thereby increasing the volume of water.

subject: Geography
q: Accelerated soil erosion results from a combination of
explanation: Accelerated soil erosion is the removal of the top layer of soil faster than it can be replaced by natural processes. This is often caused by human activities such as intensive cultivation and overgrazing. Intensive cultivation often involves the use of heavy machinery and chemicals which can compact the soil and kill beneficial organisms that help to maintain soil structure. Overgrazing by livestock can remove vegetation cover and expose the soil to erosion by wind and water.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following factors discourages population concentration in an area?
explanation: Population concentration refers to the density of population in a specific area. Factors that discourage population concentration include harsh environmental conditions that make it difficult for human habitation and economic activities. Frozen soils, for instance, make it difficult to cultivate crops, build infrastructure, and carry out other economic activities. Therefore, areas with frozen soils tend to have lower population concentrations.

subject: Geography
q: The ratio of registered births to the population of women of child-bearing age is known as the
explanation: The fertility rate is the ratio of registered births to the population of women of child-bearing age. It is a demographic measure that indicates the number of live births per 1,000 women between the ages of 15 and 49 years in a given year.

subject: Geography
q: The number of deaths, births, immigrants and emigrants over a specified time interval determines the population
explanation: Population change is determined by the number of births, deaths, immigrants, and emigrants over a specified time interval. It is the difference between the size of the population at the end and the beginning of the period.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following is mainly associated with tourism?
explanation: A holiday resort is mainly associated with tourism. It is a place where people go for holidays and relaxation. These places often have various attractions and amenities like beaches, mountains, spas, and other recreational facilities to attract tourists.

subject: Geography
q: The merging of two or more extensive urban with areas results in a
explanation: The merging of two or more extensive urban areas results in a conurbation. A conurbation is a region comprising a number of cities, large towns, and other urban areas that, through population growth and physical expansion, have merged to form one continuous urban and industrially developed area.

subject: Geography
q: Fertile plains are usually characterized by a pattern of settlement referred to as
explanation: Wide spaces are left in between settlements for farming.

subject: Geography
q: An industry is optimally located where
explanation: The essence of the choice of location of industry is to minimize cost of production to maximize profit.

subject: Geography
q: The lack of access to food in some parts of the world emanates from
explanation: Places are inaccessible because of lack of good roads & other means of transportation.

subject: Geography
q: The standard railway gauge is most common in
explanation: The standard railway gauge, which is a measure of the distance between the inner sides of the two parallel rails that make up a railway track, is most common in North America and Europe. This gauge, also known as the 'Stephenson gauge' after George Stephenson, is 1,435 mm (4 ft 8 1⁄2 in) and is used by about 60% of the world's railways.

subject: Geography
q: More than half of the world's trade is
explanation: The largest trade route (shipping) is between U.S.A and Europe. Trans-pacific trading route is becoming increasing busy because it connects Japan and Western U.S.A.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : INTERNATIONAL ECONOMIC COOPERATION IN WEST AFRICA ECOWAS

subject: Geography
q: Cane chairs, wooden masks and ornamental brass trays found in Nigeria are products of
explanation: Cane chairs, wooden masks and ornamental brass trays found in Nigeria are products of local crafts. Local crafts refer to the production of goods by artisans, using traditional methods and materials. These crafts are often handmade and reflect the culture and traditions of the area.

subject: Geography
q: Petroleum is extracted in Nigeria by
explanation: Petroleum is extracted in Nigeria by using a drilling rig. It is a machine that creates holes in the earth sub-surface. Apart from petroleum drilling, drilling rigs can also be used to drill water wells.

subject: Geography
q: In Nigeria, the major products that are taken to the markets from the south to the north are
explanation: In Nigeria, the major products that are taken to the markets from the south to the north are plantains, garri, kolanuts and palm oil. These are all products that are commonly grown and produced in the southern part of Nigeria and are transported to the north for sale.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : BROAD OUTLINE OF NIGERIA

q: The average elevation of the eastern scarpland of Nigeria ranges from
explanation: Udi-Nsuka Plateau is the Eastern Scarpland & the height is from 300m - 600m.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : BROAD OUTLINE OF NIGERIA

subject: Geography
q: The best methods of overcoming natural difficulties in Nigerian agriculture include
options: crop rotation, fire clearing and mixed cropping
explanation: The best methods of overcoming natural difficulties in Nigerian agriculture include terracing, irrigation, and research. Terracing is the creation of flat areas on mountain slopes for cultivation, which helps prevent soil erosion. Irrigation is the artificial application of water to land to assist in the production of crops. Research is important for discovering and implementing new farming techniques and technologies. The other options include practices that can be harmful to the environment, such as over-cultivation and devegetation.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : AFRICA ON BROAD OUTLINE

subject: Geography
q: The Lagos area has emerged as the most prominent destination for internal migration because it is
options: close to the coast and is densely populated
agriculturally productive and close to the coast
on a high plain and is highly industrialized
explanation: Lagos has emerged as the most prominent destination for internal migration in Nigeria because it is highly industrialized and highly urbanized. Industrialization refers to the process by which an economy is transformed from primarily agricultural to one based on the manufacturing of goods. Urbanization refers to the increasing number of people that live in urban areas. Lagos is Nigeria's largest city and its economic hub, with many industries and businesses located there.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : BROAD OUTLINE OF NIGERIA

subject: Geography
q: Inter-regional trade in Nigeria has mostly been promoted by the fact that
explanation: South West produces cocoa and kolanut, Mid West produces rubber, South East produces rubber and oil palm while North produces groundnut and cotton.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : BROAD OUTLINE OF NIGERIA

subject: Geography
q: The largest rift valley system in the world is in
explanation: The great East African rift valley system that stretches from the Red Sea to Zambia covering about 6,000km.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : AFRICA ON BROAD OUTLINE

subject: Geography
q: The Sahel region of Africa is prone to be
explanation: The Sahel region of Africa is prone to drought. The Sahel is a transitional zone in Africa between the Sahara to the north and the more fertile region to the south, known as the Sudanian Savanna. The Sahel is characterized by its semi-arid climate, which makes it prone to drought.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : AFRICA ON BROAD OUTLINE

subject: Geography
q: The Algeria's oil fields are located in
explanation: The Hassi Messaoud is a town in eastern Algeria known for its oil fields. It is the largest oil field in Algeria and has been a major contributor to the country's economy.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : AFRICA ON BROAD OUTLINE

subject: Geography
q: The major factors that account for the pattern of population distribution in West Africa are
explanation: The pattern of population distribution in West Africa is largely influenced by physical and economic factors. Physical factors include things like climate, soil fertility, and availability of water resources, while economic factors include things like job opportunities, infrastructure, and access to markets.

subject: Geography
q: The highest mountain in Africa is found in
explanation: It is Mount Kilimanjaro at 5,895m. The highest mountain in Africa is found in Kenya

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : AFRICA ON BROAD OUTLINE

subject: Geography
q: The two major plantation crops in West and East Africa respectively are
explanation: The two major plantation crops in West and East Africa are cocoa and coffee respectively. West Africa, particularly Ivory Coast and Ghana, is known for its significant production of cocoa. On the other hand, East Africa, especially Ethiopia and Kenya, is renowned for its coffee production.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : PLANTATION AGRICULTURE IN WEST AND EAST AFRICA

subject: Geography
q: The most densely populated river delta in Africa is the
explanation: The most densely populated river delta in Africa is the Nile Delta. The Nile Delta is located in Northern Egypt where the Nile River spreads out and drains into the Mediterranean Sea. It is one of the world's largest river deltas and it has supported a significant population for millennia.

subject: Geography
q: The principle of a closed traverse is that
options: the compass is read at each point in turn and as a check back bearings are taken
the compass is read and forward bearings are taken
the compass is read at each point and plotted as a check
the compass is read at each point and turned back at each point
explanation: A closed traverse in surveying is a series of connected lines, the first and last of which join in a point, forming a closed circuit. The principle of a closed traverse is that the compass is read at each point in turn and as a check, back bearings are taken. This is done to ensure the accuracy of the survey. Back bearings are the opposite direction of the forward bearings and are used to check the accuracy of the survey.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY I : ELEMENTARY SURVEYING CHAIN AND PRISMATIC

subject: Geography
q: The equatorial circumference of the earth is
explanation: The equatorial circumference of the Earth is approximately 40,075 kilometers. This is the distance around the Earth at the equator. The closest option to this value is 40,084km.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : THE EARTH AS A PLANET

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following rocks is suitable for the construction of roads and building purposes?
explanation: Granite is a type of igneous rock that is very hard and durable, making it suitable for construction purposes such as roads and buildings. It is resistant to weathering and can bear significant weight, making it ideal for such uses. Limestone can also be used in construction, but it is not as durable as granite. Basalt is also a hard rock but it is not commonly used in construction. Graphite is a form of carbon and is not suitable for construction.

subject: Geography
q: Mountains formed as a result of compressional forces are known as
explanation: Fold Mountains are formed as a result of compressional forces. When the Earth's crust is subjected to compressional forces, it buckles and folds, creating Fold Mountains. This process is known as orogeny.

subject: Geography
q: The Mediterranean Sea has a higher salinity than the oceans because
options: there is low rainfall in the region
explanation: The Mediterranean Sea has a higher salinity than the oceans because there is low rainfall in the region. Salinity refers to the saltiness or dissolved salt content of a body of water. In regions with low rainfall, there is less freshwater to dilute the salt, leading to higher salinity.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : AFRICA ON BROAD OUTLINE

subject: Geography
q: Sun-dial is used for recording the
options: position of the sun in a place
intensity of sunshine within a period of time
explanation: A sun-dial is a device that tells the time of day by the apparent position of the Sun in the sky. In the narrowest sense of the word it will consists of a flat plate, the dial and a gnomon which will throw a shadow onto the surface; lines marked onto the surface will indicate the time of day.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY I : ELEMENTARY SURVEYING CHAIN AND PRISMATIC

subject: Geography
q: The actual amount of water vapour present in the air which is expressed in grams per cubic metre is
explanation: The actual amount of water vapour present in the air which is expressed in grams per cubic metre is referred to as absolute humidity. Absolute humidity is a measure of the actual amount of water vapour in a particular sample of air: measured in grams per cubic metre. The other options refer to different aspects of humidity. Relative humidity, for example, is the ratio of the current absolute humidity to the highest possible absolute humidity.

subject: Geography
q: Pine, fir, larch and spruce are commonly found in
explanation: Pine, fir, larch and spruce are commonly found in coniferous forests. Coniferous forests are predominantly evergreen trees that bear cones, with needles rather than leaves. Deciduous forests are made up of trees that lose their leaves in the fall, equatorial forests are tropical rainforests found near the equator, and mangrove forests are found in coastal areas where the soil is loose and waterlogged.

subject: Geography
q: The major components of soil are
explanation: Water, air, mineral and organic matter are major components. Soil texture and structure are properties and not components.

subject: Geography
q: Soil forming process in the hot, humid tropics where rainfall and temperature are high throughout the year is known as
explanation: It is the only process of land formation in the hot humid region. Podzolization occurs in humid temperatures while calcification and salinization for desert and semi-desert.

subject: Geography
q: An example of an atmosphere resource is
explanation: An atmospheric resource is a resource that is derived from the atmosphere. Water, in the form of rainfall, is an example of an atmospheric resource. It is a renewable resource that is replenished by the water cycle, which includes evaporation, condensation, and precipitation.

subject: Geography
q: Igneous rocks are different from sedimentary rocks by their
explanation: Igneous rocks and sedimentary rocks are primarily differentiated by their origin and appearance. Igneous rocks are formed from the cooling and solidification of magma or lava, while sedimentary rocks are formed from the accumulation of sediments. This difference in formation leads to distinct differences in their appearance. For example, igneous rocks often have a crystalline structure due to the slow cooling of magma, while sedimentary rocks often have layers due to the deposition of sediments over time.

subject: Geography
q: The massive removal of trees and forest cover without replacement is referred to as
explanation: Deforestation refers to the process of clearing a wide area of trees. It is the removal or clearing of a forest or stand of trees where the land is thereafter converted to a non-forest use. This is different from afforestation which is the process of establishing a forest, or stand of trees, in an area where there was no forest. Bush clearing and bush burning do not necessarily involve the removal of trees and forest cover without replacement.

subject: Geography
q: The total population of a country can be obtained from its
explanation: National census is the process of counting per head every human in a particular are usually at the interval of about 10 years.

subject: Geography
q: The most significant way the components of ecosystem can be identified is that, there must be a degree of
explanation: Every component of an ecosystem has a degree of complexity.

subject: Geography
q: The major factor that affected the growth of the population of Africa during the pre-colonial times
explanation: Pre-colonial times was a period of restless inter-tribal wars as a result of struggle for ethnic survival.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following terms relates population size of land?
explanation: Density is the number of people living in an area per time divided by the land area i.e person/km2

subject: Geography
q: The problems of unemployment, housing, traffic congestion and environmental degradation are associated with
explanation: Areas of old low housing where living and social conditions.

subject: Geography
q: Rural settlements are important to urban centres because they provide
explanation: Rural settlements are important to urban centres because they provide labour. Rural areas are often the source of the workforce that migrates to urban areas for better job opportunities. This is not to say that rural areas do not also provide other resources such as food and raw materials, but in the context of the options provided, labour is the most accurate answer.

subject: Geography
q: Settlements that develop along communication routes are likely to be
explanation: Settlements that develop along communication routes are likely to be linear. This is because the development of settlements is often influenced by the availability and accessibility of transportation and communication routes. When a settlement develops along a road, railway, or river, it often takes on a linear shape, following the path of the route.

subject: Geography
q: The greatest advantage of road transportation over other modes is that the former is
explanation: The greatest advantage of road transportation over other modes is that it is more flexible. Road transportation allows for door-to-door or street-to-street services, which air, rail, and water transportation cannot provide. This flexibility makes it possible to deliver goods and services directly to specific locations, making it a preferred mode of transportation for many businesses and individuals.

subject: Geography
q: The best breeds of dairy cattle can be found in
explanation: The best breeds of dairy cattle can be found in Europe. This is because Europe has a long history of dairy farming and has developed several high producing dairy breeds such as the Holstein, Jersey, and Guernsey.

subject: Geography
q: The greatest benefit of mixed farming is that
options: the animals produce the manure for crop production
it combines the advantage of crop and animal husbandary
explanation: Since mixed farming is the cultivation of crops and rearing of animals, it combines the benefits of the two.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following is the busiest and most important world shipping route?
explanation: It is the route that connects the two most industrialized places in the world; Western Europe and Eastern USA.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following groups of countries is a major producer of rubber?
explanation: Malaysia, Liberia, and Indonesia are among the world's largest producers of rubber. Malaysia and Indonesia, in particular, are known for their vast rubber plantations. Liberia also has a significant rubber industry, with Firestone Liberia being one of the largest rubber plantations in the world.

subject: Geography
q: The greatest industrial areas of the world are
options: the Ruhr industrial region and South Africa
Western Europe and the United States of America
explanation: The greatest industrial areas of the world are indeed in Western Europe and the United States of America. These regions have a high concentration of industries due to their advanced economies, technological capabilities, and infrastructure. The Ruhr region in Germany, for example, is one of the most industrialized areas in Europe, while the United States has numerous industrial hubs such as the Rust Belt.

subject: Geography
q: The Obudu Plateau is noted for its
explanation: The Obudu Plateau is noted for its cattle ranch. The Obudu Cattle Ranch, now known as the Obudu Mountain Resort, is one of the most beautiful tourist destinations in Nigeria. It was developed in 1951 and is a popular destination for its beautiful landscapes, serene environment, and cool climate.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : AFRICA ON BROAD OUTLINE

subject: Geography
q: Livestock rearing in Nigeria is still predominantly the occupation of
explanation: Livestock rearing in Nigeria is predominantly the occupation of nomadic herders. Nomadic herding is a form of pastoralism where livestock are herded in order to find fresh pastures on which to graze. This is often the case in regions where the quality of the soil or the climate makes it impossible to grow crops consistently.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following has the highest concentration of industries in Nigeria?
explanation: It is considered to be the commercial and industrial capital of Nigeria and that is a reason it is the most populous state.

subject: Geography
q: The location of a cement industry in Ewekoro, Nigeria is largely influenced by
explanation: The location of a cement industry in Ewekoro, Nigeria is largely influenced by nearness to raw materials. Cement production requires limestone and clay, and these materials are heavy and expensive to transport. Therefore, cement factories are often located near the source of these raw materials to reduce transportation costs.

subject: Geography
q: In chain surveying, the basic principle is to measure
explanation: Chain surveying is a type of surveying that involves the measurement of distances. It is the simplest method of surveying in which only linear measurements are made in the field. The main principle of chain surveying is to provide a framework of lines of known length and direction. The angles, heights, and depths are not the primary measurements in chain surveying.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY I : ELEMENTARY SURVEYING CHAIN AND PRISMATIC

subject: Geography
q: Which of these planets completes its orbit in a much shorter space of time?
explanation: Mercury, the planet closest to the sun, completes its orbit in a much shorter space of time compared to other planets. It takes approximately 88 Earth days for Mercury to complete one full orbit around the sun. This is much shorter than the orbital periods of Saturn, Jupiter, and Earth.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : THE EARTH AS A PLANET

subject: Geography
q: Shale is a fine-textured sedimentary rock formed principally from the particles of
explanation: Shale is a fine-grained, clastic sedimentary rock composed of mud that is a mix of flakes of clay minerals and tiny fragments (silt-sized particles) of other minerals, especially quartz and calcite.

subject: Geography
q: Organic sedimentary rocks are formed from the
explanation: Organic sedimentary rocks are formed from the remains of plants and animals. These remains were once living organisms, hence the term 'organic'. They are deposited over time, often millions of years, and become compacted to form rock.

subject: Geography
q: The intrusive landform produced when magma is cooled within the earth surface is
explanation: Batholith is a massive type of plutonic rock formed as a result of solidification of molten magma within the earth crust.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following is an agent of denudation?
explanation: Wind aids the lowering of the earth surface especially in the desert where land surface is exposed to wind erosion.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following rocks is characterized by layer formation and strata?
explanation: Sedimentary rocks are characterized by layer formation and strata. They are formed from the accumulation of sediments such as sand, silt, and even organic material. Over time, these layers are compacted and cemented together to form rock. The layers or 'strata' are often visible in sedimentary rocks.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following seas has the highest salinity?
explanation: The Red Sea has the highest salinity among the given options. Salinity refers to the amount of salts dissolved in water. The Red Sea's high salinity is due to high evaporation rates, limited freshwater inflow, and its connection to the Indian Ocean which is also high in salinity.

subject: Geography
q: Some lakes are seasonal due to their location in
explanation: Some lakes are seasonal due to their location in hot deserts. In hot desert areas, lakes may form temporarily after rainfall but evaporate quickly due to the high temperatures and aridity. These are often called ephemeral lakes.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following is an element of weather?
explanation: An element of weather refers to the different factors that determine weather conditions. These include temperature, humidity, wind, and precipitation. Precipitation, which includes rain, snow, sleet, and hail, is therefore an element of weather. Thunder is a sound caused by lightning, breeze is a light wind, and latitude is a geographical coordinate, none of which are elements of weather.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: A line drawn on a map to show places of equal temperature is known as
explanation: Isotherm is for equal temperature, isobar is for equal pressure, isohyet for equal rainfall and isoline for equal value.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE

subject: Geography
q: The term used to describe the vegetation type of the Amazon Basin is
explanation: The term used to describe the vegetation type of the Amazon Basin is 'selvas'. Selvas refers to dense, generally tropical, vegetation, typically having high biodiversity. Pampas is a type of lowland area in South America, coniferous refers to trees that produce cones and have needle-like leaves, and vale is a term for a valley, none of which accurately describe the vegetation type of the Amazon Basin.

subject: Geography
q: The major factor which determines the rate of infiltration of water into the sub-soil is
explanation: Nature of rocks determine percolation rate. In rocks with small pore spaces, the rate is low.

subject: Geography
q: The level of development of the soil profile is determined by
explanation: Soil organic matter (SOM) is the organic matter component of soil, consisting of plant and animal residues at various stages of decomposition, cells and tissues of soil organisms, and substances synthesized by soil organisms.

subject: Geography
q: Volcanoes are considered natural resources because they provide
explanation: The areas of volcanic mountains have fertile soil as a result of the rock mineral washed down or weathered.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following is a component of a biotic community?
explanation: A biotic community, also known as a biological community, is a group of organisms that interact with each other in a specific environment. This includes animals, plants, fungi, and microorganisms. Therefore, 'Animals' is the correct answer. Rocks, Water, and Gases are abiotic components, meaning they are non-living physical and chemical elements in an ecosystem.

subject: Geography
q: The type of coal that burns with great heat and very little smoke is
explanation: The best type of coal is anthracite. It is the hardest with high hydrocarbon content and it is practically smokeless.

subject: Geography
q: The most serious environmental effect posed by hazardous wastes is
explanation: Air pollution spreads wider and faster than any other form of pollution.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following indices is a measure of development?
explanation: Life expectancy is a measure of development. It is an average estimate of how long a person is expected to live. This is based on the year of their birth, their current age, and other demographic factors including their gender. Higher life expectancy usually indicates better healthcare and living conditions, which are indicators of development. The other options like total population, population density, and types of building are not direct measures of development.

subject: Geography
q: The growth rate of population is determined by
explanation: The growth rate of a population is determined by the birth and death rates. It is calculated by subtracting the death rate from the birth rate. The other options like improved agriculture and technological know-how can indirectly affect the population growth rate by improving living conditions and thus potentially increasing birth rates and decreasing death rates, but they are not direct determinants of population growth rate.

subject: Geography
q: A population problem faced by some developed countries today is
explanation: In developed nations, the lower dependency class(children) is always very small while the working class(16 - 64) is always very large, so there is the problem of replacing the working class. That is why developed nations are turning to Africa for workforce.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following is a favourable condition for settlement site?
explanation: Adequate water supply is a favourable condition for settlement site. Water is a basic necessity for human survival. It is used for drinking, cooking, washing, and irrigation of farms. Therefore, areas with adequate water supply are often chosen as settlement sites. Dense natural vegetation, marshy Fadama area, and mountainous areas are not ideal for settlement because they present challenges such as difficulty in clearing the land for building and farming, poor drainage and difficult accessibility.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following countries is perennial irrigation extensively practised?
explanation: The Sudan is located in the desert and as such perennial irrigation is extensively practised.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : IRRIGATION AGRICULTURE IN THE NILE AND NIGER BASIN

subject: Geography
q: Dispersed rural settlements are most likely to be found in areas with
options: limited agricultural land and poor surface drainage
abundant agricultural land and good surface drainage
a large agricultural population and restricted accessibility
a small agricultural population and poor surface drainage
explanation: Each farmer takes portion of land for his farming and keeps a distance from another farm but if the land is limited, the farms get clustered together.

subject: Geography
q: Dairy farming is an example of intensive farming commonly practised in
explanation: Intensive farming refers to a type of agriculture, both of crop plants and of animals, with higher levels of input and output per cubic unit of agricultural land area. Denmark is known for its dairy farming due to its favourable climate and advanced technology in dairy farming.

subject: Geography
q: As a country, a smaller percentage of the workforce tend to be employed in
explanation: When a large percentage of the workforce is engaged in agriculture, such a nation is termed a developing nation.

subject: Geography
q: Developing countries mostly engage in primary economic activities because of
explanation: Primary economic activities are those that involve the extraction of raw materials from the earth. These activities are the most basic form of economic activity and are often the first to develop in a country. Developing countries often have a high percentage of their workforce engaged in these activities due to a lack of development in other sectors of the economy.

subject: Geography
q: High annual rainfall of at least 1520mm well distributed throughout the year with the heaviest during growing season is most suitable for the production of
explanation: Rice is the crop that requires high annual rainfall of at least 1520mm well distributed throughout the year with the heaviest during growing season. Rice is a water-intensive crop and thrives in areas with high rainfall.

subject: Geography
q: The main factor influencing the location of manufacturing industries in West Africa is
explanation: The main factor influencing the location of manufacturing industries in West Africa is nearness to raw materials. Industries are usually located near the source of raw materials to reduce the cost of transportation and ensure a steady supply of materials.

subject: Geography
q: An agricultural practice that allows a wide range of crops to be grown without fallowing is
explanation: It allows wide range of crops without fallowing. A land is divided into different portions with each producing different crops and is subjected to rotation yearly.

subject: Geography
q: Arable farming and animal husbandry widely practised in Britain and many parts of Europe is referred to as
explanation: Arable farming and animal husbandry widely practised in Britain and many parts of Europe is referred to as mixed farming. Mixed farming is a type of farming which involves both the growing of crops as well as the raising of livestock. The aim is to increase income through different sources and to complement the enterprises in terms of labor utilization and production.

subject: Geography
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question


The area marked P is
explanation: The highlands of Western Nigeria. The area marked P is Western Uplands.

subject: Geography
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The area marked N is over
explanation: Adamawa Plateau is between 1,800 and 2,400m above sea level.

subject: Geography
q: The type of vegetation found on the Mambila Plateau is
explanation: Montane Vegetation is found on the high plateau. The type of vegetation found on the Mambila Plateau is high forest.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following best explains the reason for the decrease in the practice of bush fallowing in Nigeria?
explanation: The decrease in the practice of bush fallowing in Nigeria is best explained by the rapid growth of population. Bush fallowing is a traditional method of farming where a piece of land is cleared and cultivated for a period of time and then left to regain its fertility naturally. However, with the rapid growth of population, there is increased demand for land for housing and other uses, reducing the amount of land available for bush fallowing.

subject: Geography
q: Secondary economic activities are those that involve the
explanation: Secondary economic activities involve the processing of raw materials into finished goods. This includes activities such as manufacturing, processing, and construction. The other options involve either the distribution or packaging of goods, which are considered tertiary activities as they do not involve the transformation of raw materials.

subject: Geography
q: The reason for seasonal migration of nomadic herdsmen is
options: for the search of water and good grazing
because of the inability of government to provide shelter
due to high rainfall in some regions
explanation: Nomadic herdsmen migrate seasonally primarily in search of water and good grazing for their livestock. This is because the availability of these resources varies with the seasons. The other options do not accurately describe the primary reasons for the seasonal migration of nomadic herdsmen.

subject: Geography
q: The main industry associated with the Ruhr Valley region is
explanation: The Ruhr Valley region in Germany is known for its iron and steel industry. It is one of the largest industrial regions in Europe and has a high concentration of heavy industry, including iron and steel production. The other options do not accurately represent the main industry associated with the Ruhr Valley region.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following factors influenced the growth of the Ruhr Valley?
explanation: The Ruhr Valley in Germany is known for its industrial development rather than its agricultural potential. The region is rich in coal, which was a key factor in its industrialization during the 19th and 20th centuries. Therefore, the factor that influenced the growth of the Ruhr Valley is 'Industrialization'.

subject: Geography
q: The largest producer of timber in Africa is
explanation: Cote d'Ivoire is the largest producer of timber in Africa. The country has a significant forest cover and has a well-developed timber industry. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Cote'd Ivoire'.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : LUMBERING IN EQUATORIAL AFRICA

subject: Geography
q: The relatively low level of rail transport development in tropical Africa has
options: increased the rate of migration from the sea
explanation: The relatively low level of rail transport development in tropical Africa has placed undue pressure on roads. This is because the lack of a well-developed rail network means that most goods and passengers have to be transported by road, which can lead to congestion and increased wear and tear on the roads. Therefore, the correct answer is 'placed undue pressure on roads'.

subject: Geography
q: The high population density in the Nile Valley is as a result of
explanation: Perennial irrigation system where water is stored in dams for the planting season.

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following population characteristics is best related to African countries?
options: Low birth rate and low death rate
High birth rate and low death rate
Low birth rate and high death rate
High birth rate and high death rate
explanation: Religion and culture encourage high birth rate while improved medical care has reduced death rate leading to high population in Africa.

subject: Geography
q: The feature marked X - Y is a/an
explanation:
The featured marked X - Y is a dry valley. This is because the section XY contains low lands that exiist between two hills.
N.B: It should be noted that a valley can be defined as a low land that exist between two hills.

subject: Geography
q: River Owen is a/an
explanation:
River Owerri is a tributary of River Oshe
N.B: A tributary is a natural water stream that flows into a larger river or other body of water.

A → Confluence
X → Tributary
Y → Tributary
Z → Large River

subject: Geography
q: Sugar cane can easily be grown in the
explanation: Sugar cane can easily be grown in the south-western part of the map. This is because the south-western part of the map contains the necessary materials (climatic and environmental) needed for the cultivation of sugar cane. We have " grasslands, river and areas liable to flooding" in the South-Western part of the map.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : PLANTATION AGRICULTURE IN WEST AND EAST AFRICA

q: OFOT is a
explanation: OFOT is a nodal town as it is an area organized around a node, or focal point defined by interactions of connections.

topics.title: REGIONAL GEOGRAPHY : GEOGRAPHICAL REGIONS OF NIGERIA

q: The long stretch of highland in the northern part of the map is a
explanation:
The long stretch of highland in the northern part of the map is a ridge.
N.B: A ridge or mountain ridge are geological features consisting of a chain of mountains or hills that form a continuous elevated crest for some distance.

subject: Geography
q: The bearing of Agaba from Deog is about
explanation: The bearing of Agaba from Deog is about 140°.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY I : ELEMENTARY SURVEYING CHAIN AND PRISMATIC

explanation: The road network on the map extends away from, i.e avoids highlands

subject: Geography
q: Which of these statements is true about the relief of the map?
options: The central area is higher than other areas
The northern area is generally low in elevation
The central area is lower than other areas
The southern area is generally low in elevation
explanation: The relief of the map is such that the central area is lower than other areas.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY I : MAP READING AND INTERPRETATION

subject: Geography
q: Which of the following is not a likely occupation of the people in Agaba?
explanation:
We have many regions with scattered cultivation, hence, farming is a likely occupation. We have reliable road networks and settlements that are connected, hence, trading is a likely occupation. Also, there are grasslands, hence cattle rearing is a likely occupation.
There is only one region (OFOT) in the map where mining is done, hence mining cannot be a likely occupation of the people in Agaba.

subject: Geography
q: OFOT can be described as a
explanation: OFOT is the only place where mining takes place, hence we may describe OFOT as a mining settlement.

subject: Geography
q:
Use the climate data below to answer this question
MonthJFMAMJJASOND
Temperature (oC222630323329282627252828
Rainfall (mm)23413641222083071401200

The climatic data above can best be represented by
explanation: The climatic data provided, which includes monthly temperature and rainfall, can best be represented by bar and line graphs. Bar and line graphs are ideal for showing changes over time, such as monthly changes in temperature and rainfall. A bar graph can be used to represent rainfall, with the height of the bar indicating the amount of rainfall. A line graph can be used to represent temperature, with the position of the line indicating the temperature.

topics.title: PRATICAL GEOGRAPHY II : WEATHER AND CLIMATE
